layout-container--docs.name: Docs
layout-container--docs.title: Layout/Container
layout-container--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Container.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A bounded application workspace with responsive page gutters."
/>

# Container

`Container` centres application content in a full-width workspace, limits it to the Breeze maximum width, and applies mobile-first page gutters.

## Import

```tsx
import { Container } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use one `Container` around page-level content that should share the standard maximum width and gutters. Use `Stack`, `Inline`, or `Grid` inside it to arrange content.

Do not use `Container` for a visually bounded panel; use `Surface` or `Card` instead. Do not nest containers merely to add spacing, because their maximum width and automatic inline margins are page-level behaviour.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Container, Stack, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function AccountPage() {
  return (
    <Container>
      <Stack gap="xl">
        <Typography as="h1" level="heading-xl">
          Account
        </Typography>
        <Typography>Manage your account details.</Typography>
      </Stack>
    </Container>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ResponsiveWorkspace} />

## Responsive gutters

`padding` accepts one spacing value or an object with a required `base` value and optional `sm`, `md`, and `lg` overrides. Breakpoints are mobile-first: `sm` starts at 681 px, `md` at 901 px, and `lg` at 1181 px. The default is `{ base: 'md', sm: 'xl', md: 'xxl', lg: 'page' }`.

```tsx
<Container padding={{ base: 'sm', md: 'page' }}>Page content</Container>
```

Spacing values are `none`, `xs`, `sm`, `control`, `compact`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xxl`, and `page`. Prefer the default gutters unless a documented page composition needs another token.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility and behaviour

`Container` also accepts relevant native `div` attributes, including `id`, `aria-*`, and `data-*`; inline `style` is intentionally unavailable. It has no loading, disabled, invalid, empty, controlled, or read-only state.

`Container` is a neutral `div`: it adds no landmark, label, focus, or keyboard behaviour. Put semantic elements such as `main`, `nav`, and headings inside it. If the container itself is labelled, ensure the label communicates a real relationship rather than presentation. Content order remains the authored DOM order at every breakpoint and direction.

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `className` to recreate arbitrary page gutters when `padding` covers the requirement.
- Do not assume `Container` supplies a `main` landmark.
- Do not reorder children visually at breakpoints.

## Related components

Use `Stack` for vertical flow, `Grid` for responsive columns, `Center` for two-axis centring, and `Surface` for a coloured or bordered inset region.
layout-container--docs.summary: A bounded application workspace with responsive page gutters.

date-and-time-datefield--docs.name: Docs
date-and-time-datefield--docs.title: Date and Time/DateField
date-and-time-datefield--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DateField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Locale-aware segmented entry for one calendar date, with stable YYYY-MM-DD values, native form participation, validation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DateField

`DateField` edits one calendar date through locale-ordered day, month and year segments while keeping the application value a stable `YYYY-MM-DD` string.

## Import

```tsx
import { DateField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DateField` when users should type or precisely adjust a known date. Use `DatePicker` when a calendar is the primary way to choose one date, `Calendar` for an always-visible calendar, `DateRangePicker` for an inclusive period, and `DateTimePicker` for an instant that includes a time and offset. Do not use a date field for free-form date text or timestamps.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DateField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DateField.Root
  defaultValue="2026-07-20"
  minValue="2026-07-20"
  name="reviewDate"
>
  <DateField.Label>Review date</DateField.Label>
  <DateField.Input />
  <DateField.Description>
    Enter a date on or after 20 July.
  </DateField.Description>
</DateField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SegmentKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                    | Purpose                                                                    |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DateField.Root`        | Owns date state, constraints, validation and the hidden native date input. |
| `DateField.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the segmented input.                           |
| `DateField.Input`       | Locale-aware editable day, month and year segments.                        |
| `DateField.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the field.                             |
| `DateField.Error`       | Validation message associated with an invalid field.                       |

Keep every part inside `Root`. Provide a visible `Label`; use `Description` for format-independent guidance and `Error` for a specific correction.

## Values, state and callbacks

Dates are literal Gregorian ISO calendar strings such as `'2026-07-20'`; an empty value is `null`. A date contains no time or time zone. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next complete `YYYY-MM-DD` string or `null` when the editable value is cleared.

`minValue`, `maxValue` and the date passed to `isDateUnavailable` use the same format. The callback returning `true` prevents that date from being selected. Constraints do not replace application validation: set `invalid` and render `Error` when submitted data is unacceptable.

With `name`, the hidden native date input participates in form submission; `form` associates it with an external form. `required` supplies required semantics. `disabled` prevents focus and editing. `readOnly` preserves an inspectable controlled value without allowing changes. There is no loading state; disable an enclosing workflow or show adjacent progress without replacing the label.

## Keyboard, accessibility and internationalisation

- Tab enters and leaves the segmented control. Arrow keys move between or increment the locale-ordered segments; numeric typing edits the focused segment, and Backspace or Delete clears editable content.
- Do not put a literal format such as “DD/MM/YYYY” in the label: segment order and spoken instructions follow the `BreezeProvider` BCP 47 locale.
- The visible label is required for understandable segment names. Description and error text are associated automatically.
- `invalid`, `required`, `disabled` and `readOnly` are exposed to assistive technology; do not communicate them only through colour.
- The input follows provider direction, including right-to-left layouts. Keep the public value in `YYYY-MM-DD` order regardless of visual order.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not pass JavaScript `Date`, `CalendarDate`, locale-formatted strings or offset date-times. Do not combine `defaultValue` with `value`, omit `onChange` from a mutable controlled field, or make an uncontrolled field read-only. Use `TimeField` beside this component only when the values are intentionally independent; use `DateTimePicker` when they represent one instant.

date-and-time-datepicker--docs.title: Date and Time/DatePicker
date-and-time-datepicker--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DatePicker.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Full-width labelled date control with a locale-formatted trigger and calendar popover, stable YYYY-MM-DD values, form integration, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DatePicker

`DatePicker` selects one calendar date from a compact full-width control and an accessible calendar popover.

## Import

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DatePicker` when a labelled form field should open a calendar and exact keyboard entry is not the primary task.

## When not to use

Use `DateField` for direct segmented entry, `Calendar` for an always-visible grid, `DateRangePicker` for a period, and `DateTimePicker` for an instant with time and offset.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DatePicker.Root defaultValue="2026-07-20" name="reviewDate">
  <DatePicker.Label>Review date</DatePicker.Label>
  <DatePicker.Group>
    <DatePicker.Input />
    <DatePicker.Trigger />
  </DatePicker.Group>
  <DatePicker.Popover>
    <DatePicker.Calendar />
  </DatePicker.Popover>
</DatePicker.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.CalendarSelection} />

## Anatomy

| Part                     | Purpose                                                                                              |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DatePicker.Root`        | Owns stable date state, form semantics, constraints and overlay coordination.                        |
| `DatePicker.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the complete picker.                                                     |
| `DatePicker.Group`       | Full-width coherent control containing input state and trigger.                                      |
| `DatePicker.Input`       | Hidden date-state input required by the compound picker.                                             |
| `DatePicker.Trigger`     | Displays a locale-formatted long date or the provider’s “select date” message and opens the popover. |
| `DatePicker.Popover`     | Positioned, dismissible calendar overlay; defaults to logical `bottom end`.                          |
| `DatePicker.Calendar`    | Complete month navigation and single-date grid.                                                      |
| `DatePicker.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the picker.                                                      |
| `DatePicker.Error`       | Validation message associated with invalid state.                                                    |

Keep `Input` and `Trigger` inside `Group`, and `Calendar` inside `Popover`. Every part belongs inside `Root`. The hidden `Input` is structural; do not replace it with a native input or expose it visually.

## Values, state and form behaviour

The value is a stable `YYYY-MM-DD` string such as `'2026-07-20'`; empty state is `null`. It represents a calendar day with no time or zone. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for an immutable picker. `onChange` receives the selected ISO date or `null`.

`minValue`, `maxValue` and `isDateUnavailable(date)` use `YYYY-MM-DD`. Returning `true` marks a day unavailable. `name` submits the stable value through the native form integration and `form` associates it with an external form. `required`, `invalid`, `disabled` and `readOnly` expose field semantics; pair `invalid` with a specific `Error`. An empty trigger uses the translated provider message. Loading is application-owned.

## Keyboard, overlays and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the trigger; Enter, Space or the platform activation key opens the calendar. Escape dismisses it and returns focus to the trigger.
- In the calendar, arrow keys navigate days, Page Up and Page Down change month, and Enter or Space selects an available date and closes the popover.
- Always render `Label`. `Description` and `Error` are associated with the complete field.
- Trigger text, weekday names, month headings, week layout and announcements follow the provider locale. The stable value never changes format.
- Popover placement uses logical `start` and `end`; direction and navigation icons adapt to right-to-left layouts.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not omit structural parts, make the hidden `Input` visible, pass date objects or localised strings, or use the visual trigger text as stored data. Use `DateField` when users must type dates efficiently, `DateRangePicker` for an inclusive range, and `FormattedDateTime` for read-only output.

date-and-time-daterangepicker--docs.title: Date and Time/DateRangePicker
date-and-time-daterangepicker--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DateRangePicker.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Compound labelled form control for selecting an inclusive date range as stable YYYY-MM-DD start and end strings, with segmented inputs, a range-calendar popover, paired native form values, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DateRangePicker

`DateRangePicker` combines start and end date segments with a calendar popover for selecting one inclusive date range.

## Import

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DateRangePicker` for a compact labelled period field where users benefit from both exact segment editing and calendar comparison.

## When not to use

Use `RangeCalendar` when the grid should remain visible, `DatePicker` for one day, and separate `DateField` controls when the two dates are not one range.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DateRangePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DateRangePicker.Root
  defaultValue={{ start: '2026-07-20', end: '2026-07-24' }}
  startName="periodStart"
  endName="periodEnd"
>
  <DateRangePicker.Label>Dates</DateRangePicker.Label>
  <DateRangePicker.Group>
    <DateRangePicker.StartInput />
    <DateRangePicker.Separator />
    <DateRangePicker.EndInput />
    <DateRangePicker.Trigger />
  </DateRangePicker.Group>
  <DateRangePicker.Popover>
    <DateRangePicker.Calendar />
  </DateRangePicker.Popover>
</DateRangePicker.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.UncontrolledRange} />

## Anatomy

| Part                          | Purpose                                                                                    |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `DateRangePicker.Root`        | Owns inclusive range state, constraints, validation, form values and overlay coordination. |
| `DateRangePicker.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the complete range field.                                      |
| `DateRangePicker.Group`       | Coherent start input, separator, end input and trigger control.                            |
| `DateRangePicker.StartInput`  | Locale-aware segmented inclusive start date.                                               |
| `DateRangePicker.Separator`   | Decorative logical-direction arrow between endpoints.                                      |
| `DateRangePicker.EndInput`    | Locale-aware segmented inclusive end date.                                                 |
| `DateRangePicker.Trigger`     | Opens the range-calendar popover.                                                          |
| `DateRangePicker.Popover`     | Positioned dismissible range-calendar overlay.                                             |
| `DateRangePicker.Calendar`    | Complete range calendar with navigation and date cells.                                    |
| `DateRangePicker.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the range.                                             |
| `DateRangePicker.Error`       | Validation message associated with invalid state.                                          |

Preserve the part order shown above. The separator is hidden from assistive technology because start and end inputs already express endpoint meaning. Keep every part inside `Root`.

## Range, callbacks and forms

The value is `{ start: 'YYYY-MM-DD', end: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }`, with inclusive endpoints, or `null` when empty. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next complete stable range or `null`.

`startName` and `endName` must be provided together. Breeze submits two hidden native values and resets uncontrolled state with its form. `form` can associate both with an external form. There is no single `name` because the endpoints are distinct values.

`minValue` and `maxValue` are inclusive `YYYY-MM-DD` boundaries. `isDateUnavailable(date, anchorDate)` receives the candidate date and current selection anchor. By default an unavailable date blocks a range crossing it; set `allowsNonContiguousRanges` only when the domain explicitly permits that meaning. `required`, `invalid`, `disabled` and `readOnly` expose state; render `Error` for invalid data. Loading is application-owned.

## Keyboard, accessibility and direction

- Tab moves through the compound field and trigger. Arrow keys move between or increment start/end segments; numeric typing edits focused segments.
- Enter or Space opens the popover from `Trigger`; Escape closes it and restores focus. Calendar arrow keys navigate and Enter or Space chooses range endpoints.
- Always render `Label`. Start and end segments receive distinct accessible endpoint names; `Description` and `Error` apply to the complete field.
- Locale controls segment order, weekday names, week layout and announcements while stored values stay ISO ordered.
- `Separator` mirrors in right-to-left direction without swapping semantic `start` and `end`. Popover placement uses logical direction.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not reverse start and end for right-to-left layouts, use exclusive-end semantics, provide only one form name, pass date objects or localised strings, or treat non-contiguous ranges as a validation shortcut. Use `RangeCalendar` for a persistent surface and `DateTimePicker` when selecting one instant rather than two calendar days.

date-and-time-datetimepicker--docs.title: Date and Time/DateTimePicker
date-and-time-datetimepicker--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DateTimePicker.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Compound date-and-time picker for an unambiguous instant, using ISO 8601 values with an explicit offset and provider-zone display, segmented editing, a calendar-plus-time popover, form semantics, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DateTimePicker

`DateTimePicker` edits one unambiguous instant through locale-aware date and time segments plus a calendar-and-time popover.

## Import

```tsx
import { DateTimePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DateTimePicker` when the value represents a real instant whose date, time and UTC offset must travel together. Use `DatePicker` for a calendar day, `TimeField` for a zone-free wall-clock time, and `FormattedDateTime` for display only. Do not use it for durations or a local date-time that intentionally has no offset.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DateTimePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DateTimePicker.Root
  defaultValue="2026-07-20T14:30:00+01:00"
  name="appointment"
>
  <DateTimePicker.Label>Date and time</DateTimePicker.Label>
  <DateTimePicker.Group>
    <DateTimePicker.Input />
    <DateTimePicker.Trigger />
  </DateTimePicker.Group>
  <DateTimePicker.Popover>
    <DateTimePicker.Calendar />
  </DateTimePicker.Popover>
</DateTimePicker.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ExplicitOffset} />

## Anatomy

| Part                         | Purpose                                                                                            |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DateTimePicker.Root`        | Owns instant state, provider-zone conversion, boundaries, form semantics and overlay coordination. |
| `DateTimePicker.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the complete field.                                                    |
| `DateTimePicker.Group`       | Coherent segmented input and calendar-trigger control.                                             |
| `DateTimePicker.Input`       | Editable locale-aware date and minute segments with informative zone text.                         |
| `DateTimePicker.Trigger`     | Opens the complete calendar-and-time popover.                                                      |
| `DateTimePicker.Popover`     | Positioned dismissible dialog; defaults to logical `bottom end`.                                   |
| `DateTimePicker.Calendar`    | Complete single-date calendar followed by a labelled segmented time field.                         |
| `DateTimePicker.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the picker.                                                    |
| `DateTimePicker.Error`       | Validation message associated with invalid state.                                                  |

Keep `Input` and `Trigger` inside `Group`, and `Calendar` inside `Popover`. The time input inside `Calendar` is Breeze-owned; do not add a second `TimeField`.

## Instant values and time zones

Values are ISO 8601 strings with an explicit offset, for example `'2026-07-20T14:30:00+01:00'` or `'2026-07-20T13:30:00Z'`. Offset-free values are rejected. Empty state is `null`. The provider `timeZone` is an IANA zone such as `Europe/London`; it controls displayed calendar/time segments and daylight-saving behaviour. If omitted it defaults to `UTC` for this picker.

The input offset identifies the instant. Editing emits a normalised value with seconds and the provider zone’s offset, such as `'2026-07-20T14:31:00+01:00'`. Persist the callback value, not visible segment text. `minValue` and `maxValue` are inclusive explicit-offset instants and can use offsets different from the current display zone.

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next explicit-offset value or `null`. `name` and `form` provide native form association. `required`, `invalid`, `disabled` and `readOnly` expose state; pair invalid state with `Error`. Loading is application-owned.

## Keyboard, accessibility and direction

- Tab reaches the compound input and trigger. Arrow keys move between or increment date/time segments; numeric typing edits a segment and Backspace or Delete clears it.
- Activate `Trigger` with Enter or Space. Escape dismisses the popover and returns focus. Calendar arrow keys navigate days; Enter or Space selects. The popover time segments use the same segmented-field keys.
- Always render `Label`. The zone suffix is informative and deliberately not another focus stop.
- `Calendar` includes the provider’s translated “select time” label; `Popover` uses the translated “select date and time” accessible name unless `aria-label` is supplied.
- Locale controls segment order, hour cycle, weekday names and announcements. Logical overlay placement and navigation adapt to right-to-left direction.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not pass a JavaScript `Date`, omit the offset, append the provider zone name to the value, or treat the provider zone as the stored instant. Test daylight-saving transitions in the configured IANA zone. Use `DatePicker` plus `TimeField` only when the values are intentionally independent; use `FormattedDateTime` for read-only output.
date-and-time-datetimepicker--docs.summary: Compound date-and-time picker for an unambiguous instant, using ISO 8601 values with an explicit offset and provider-zone display, segmented editing, a calendar-plus-time popover, form semantics, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state.

data-display-descriptionlist--docs.title: Data Display/DescriptionList
data-display-descriptionlist--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DescriptionList.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Responsive native description list for presenting labelled term-and-description metadata."
/>

# DescriptionList

`DescriptionList` pairs terms with their descriptions using native `dl`, `dt`, and `dd` semantics.

```tsx
import { DescriptionList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for read-only facts, attributes, or summary metadata. Use [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs) for a sequence, [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs) for multiple comparable records, or form components when values are editable.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { DescriptionList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function RecordDetails() {
  return (
    <DescriptionList.Root>
      <DescriptionList.Item>
        <DescriptionList.Term>Status</DescriptionList.Term>
        <DescriptionList.Description>Ready</DescriptionList.Description>
      </DescriptionList.Item>
      <DescriptionList.Item>
        <DescriptionList.Term>Reference</DescriptionList.Term>
        <DescriptionList.Description>AB-123</DescriptionList.Description>
      </DescriptionList.Item>
    </DescriptionList.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ResponsivePairs} />

## Anatomy

- `DescriptionList.Root` renders the native `dl` and stacks at narrow widths.
- `DescriptionList.Item` renders a visual grouping `div` for one or more pairs.
- `DescriptionList.Term` renders `dt`.
- `DescriptionList.Description` renders `dd` associated with the preceding term.

Keep each term next to its description. Multiple descriptions may follow a term, and multiple terms may precede one description where that is genuinely the semantic relationship.

## Behaviour and accessibility

The component is read-only and non-interactive: it has no controlled state, callbacks, keyboard behaviour, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error state. Translate both terms and descriptions. Logical spacing and the responsive grid support both left-to-right and right-to-left layouts. Format dates and numbers before passing them as content.

Every part supports the relevant native semantic-element attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; inline `style` is intentionally excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not reproduce the relationship with generic `div` elements.
- Do not use terms as visual headings for unrelated sections.
- Do not place editable controls here when a labelled form is required.
- Do not force columns with custom layout; the built-in layout adapts at the Breeze breakpoint.

## Related components

[List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs), [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs), and [Card](?path=/docs/data-display-card--docs) cover sequences, comparable records, and contained summaries.
data-display-descriptionlist--docs.summary: Responsive native description list for presenting labelled term-and-description metadata.

stories.snippet:
function ControlledFocusAndDismissal() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <Dialog.Root onOpenChange={setOpen} open={open}>
      <Dialog.Trigger>Open controlled dialog</Dialog.Trigger>
      <Dialog.Content>
        <Dialog.Title>Controlled settings</Dialog.Title>
        <Dialog.Description>
          Focus is contained until this modal closes.
        </Dialog.Description>
        <Stack gap="xl">
          <TextField.Root>
            <TextField.Label>Display name</TextField.Label>
            <TextField.Input autoFocus />
          </TextField.Root>
          <ButtonGroup
            align="end"
            orientation={{ base: 'vertical', sm: 'horizontal' }}
          >
            <Dialog.Close>Save and close</Dialog.Close>
          </ButtonGroup>
        </Stack>
      </Dialog.Content>
    </Dialog.Root>
  );
}

primitives-overlays-dialog--docs.name: Docs
primitives-overlays-dialog--docs.title: Primitives/Overlays/Dialog
primitives-overlays-dialog--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Dialog.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A modal task surface with required accessible title and description, contained focus, explicit close actions, and controlled or uncontrolled open state."
/>

# Dialog

`Dialog` interrupts the current page with a focused modal task or decision that must be completed or dismissed.

## Import

```tsx
import { Dialog } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for a short self-contained task that needs the user's immediate attention.

## When not to use

Use `AlertDialog` for a consequential decision, `Drawer` for a longer edge-mounted task, `Popover` for lightweight contextual controls, and inline content when interruption is unnecessary.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Dialog, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Dialog.Root>
  <Dialog.Trigger>Edit details</Dialog.Trigger>
  <Dialog.Content>
    <Dialog.Title>Edit details</Dialog.Title>
    <Dialog.Description>
      Review the information before closing.
    </Dialog.Description>
    <TextField.Root>
      <TextField.Label>Name</TextField.Label>
      <TextField.Input autoFocus />
    </TextField.Root>
    <Dialog.Close>Close</Dialog.Close>
  </Dialog.Content>
</Dialog.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ControlledFocusAndDismissal} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Dialog.Root`        | Coordinates open state, trigger focus, dismissal, and restoration. |
| `Dialog.Trigger`     | Semantic button that opens the dialog.                             |
| `Dialog.Content`     | Backdrop, modal focus scope, scroll boundary, and dialog surface.  |
| `Dialog.Title`       | Required heading used as the accessible name.                      |
| `Dialog.Description` | Required explanatory text used as the accessible description.      |
| `Dialog.Close`       | Semantic action that closes and restores trigger focus.            |

Keep `Title` and `Description` inside `Content`. Put the most appropriate first focus target on an interactive descendant with `autoFocus`; otherwise React Aria chooses a safe target.

## Open state, dismissal, and feedback

Use `defaultOpen` for uncontrolled state, `open` with `onOpenChange` for controlled state, or `open` with `readOnly` for intentionally immutable state. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean for trigger activation, outside dismissal, Escape, and close actions. In read-only mode those interactions request no state change because no callback exists.

`dismissible` defaults to `true` and allows outside pointer dismissal. `keyboardDismissDisabled` defaults to `false`; set it only when Escape would be unsafe and provide an obvious close action. Loading, disabled, invalid, empty, and error states belong to the controls or feedback inside the dialog. Do not disable every dismissal path during an indefinite load.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and callback semantics

Trigger and Close follow the public Button interaction vocabulary: use `onAction`, not `onClick`. Content accepts relevant dialog HTML and ARIA attributes. Breeze owns overlay role, focus containment, inline styling, and dismissal semantics.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

Activating Trigger opens the modal and moves focus inside. Tab and Shift+Tab remain within it. Escape dismisses unless disabled, Close dismisses explicitly, and focus returns to the trigger. Give every dialog a concise Title and meaningful Description; visible content elsewhere does not replace these parts. Long translated text and narrow viewports scroll inside the bounded surface. The provider propagates locale and direction into the portal.

Do not nest unrelated dialogs, omit a dismissal action, put primary page navigation in a dialog, or use it for passive notifications. Related components: `AlertDialog`, `Drawer`, `Popover`, `ConfirmationDialog`, and `Toast`.
primitives-overlays-dialog--docs.summary: A modal task surface with required accessible title and description, contained focus, explicit close actions, and controlled or uncontrolled open state.

primitives-navigation-disclosure--docs.title: Primitives/Navigation/Disclosure
primitives-navigation-disclosure--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Disclosure.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A single labelled disclosure for collapsible supporting content with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and disabled state."
/>

# Disclosure

`Disclosure` reveals or hides one section of supporting content from a semantic heading button.

## Import

```tsx
import { Disclosure } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for optional details attached to one context.

## When not to use

Use `Accordion` for several coordinated sections, `Dialog` for a modal task, and permanently visible content when hiding it would impede completion or discovery.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Disclosure } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Disclosure.Root defaultOpen>
  <Disclosure.Trigger>Advanced details</Disclosure.Trigger>
  <Disclosure.Panel role="region">Additional information</Disclosure.Panel>
</Disclosure.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Controlled} />

## Anatomy and state

| Part                 | Purpose                                          |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `Disclosure.Root`    | Coordinates expansion and disabled state.        |
| `Disclosure.Trigger` | Heading button that toggles the panel.           |
| `Disclosure.Panel`   | Collapsible content associated with the trigger. |

Use `defaultOpen` for uncontrolled state, `open` with `onOpenChange` for controlled state, or `open` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean. `disabled` prevents the trigger changing state; an already open panel remains readable. There are no loading, invalid, empty, or error states.

`headingLevel` defaults to `3` and should follow document hierarchy. Panel `role` defaults to `'group'`; use `'region'` sparingly for significant sections.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Parts accept `className`, relevant HTML/ARIA attributes, and element refs. Inline styles and native click/change handlers are excluded where Breeze owns behaviour.

## Accessibility and common mistakes

Tab focuses the trigger; Enter or Space toggles it. Keep the trigger meaningful independently and do not place another interactive control inside it. Expansion state and trigger/panel association are supplied automatically. Translate the trigger without changing application state; wrapping and logical alignment follow direction.

Do not combine controlled and uncontrolled props, use a disclosure to conceal required form errors, or add a second panel under the same trigger. Related components: `Accordion`, `Dialog`, `Popover`, and `Typography`.
primitives-navigation-disclosure--docs.summary: A single labelled disclosure for collapsible supporting content with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and disabled state.

          adjacent={{ inlineEndOffset: 768 }}
          chrome="soft"

primitives-overlays-drawer--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Drawer.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A responsive edge-mounted modal task surface with logical placement, canonical widths, framed or application-owned chrome, nested layering, and trigger-owned or external state."
/>

# Drawer

`Drawer` presents a focused modal task from a logical viewport edge while preserving page context behind it.

## Import

```tsx
import { Drawer } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for a task or detail view that benefits from more vertical space than a dialog and retains useful page context.

## When not to use

Use `Dialog` for compact centred tasks, `Popover` for lightweight contextual controls, and route navigation for complex or bookmarkable workflows.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Drawer } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Drawer.Root>
  <Drawer.Trigger>Edit item</Drawer.Trigger>
  <Drawer.Content placement={{ base: 'bottom', md: 'end' }} size="medium">
    <Drawer.Title>Edit item</Drawer.Title>
    <Drawer.Description>Update the selected item's details.</Drawer.Description>
    <Drawer.Close>Close</Drawer.Close>
  </Drawer.Content>
</Drawer.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ResponsivePlacementAndNested} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                             |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Drawer.Root`        | Coordinates trigger-owned or externally controlled open state.      |
| `Drawer.Trigger`     | Opens a standard compound drawer.                                   |
| `Drawer.Content`     | Responsive modal edge surface, focus scope, frame, and scroll body. |
| `Drawer.Title`       | Required accessible name and framed heading.                        |
| `Drawer.Description` | Required accessible description and framed supporting text.         |
| `Drawer.Close`       | Explicit close action for the current layer.                        |

With `chrome="default"` or `"soft"`, Breeze moves Title and Description into a fixed header, adds a localised icon close action, and places remaining children in a scrolling body. `chrome="none"` leaves all children unframed for an application-owned shell; the application must compose its own visible Close while retaining Title and Description.

## State, placement, and layers

Standard roots support uncontrolled `defaultOpen`, controlled `open` with `onOpenChange`, or immutable `open` with `readOnly`. For an application-owned trigger outside the compound root, set `triggerless`, `open`, and `onOpenChange`; `defaultOpen` and read-only mode are unavailable. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean.

`placement` accepts `'start'`, `'end'`, or `'bottom'`, or a mobile-first object such as `{ base: 'bottom', md: 'end' }`. Breakpoints are `sm` 681px, `md` 901px, and `lg` 1181px. Start and end are logical and mirror in RTL. `size` is `default` (36rem), `medium` (38rem), or `wide` (48rem); bottom drawers remain full width.

`adjacent={{ inlineEndOffset: number }}` reserves desktop inline-end viewport space for an existing layer and uses the canonical adjacent width. It is for deliberately nested desktop drawers, not arbitrary layout. `scrollResetKey` resets the framed body to its start when the key changes.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Trigger and Close accept their public Button attributes; Content, Title, and Description accept relevant semantic attributes and refs. Breeze owns overlay positioning, role, inline styling, and focus behaviour.

## Accessibility, direction, and limitations

Opening contains focus; the framed header close receives initial focus. Tab stays within the current layer, Escape closes the top layer first unless disabled, and focus restores to that layer's trigger. Nested drawers must remain shallow and each needs its own Title and Description. Portals inherit provider locale and direction. Motion respects reduced-motion preferences.

Drawer has no root loading, invalid, empty, or error state; express those inside the scroll body and retain a dismissal path. Avoid using `chrome="none"` without a close action, fixed pixel offsets that do not match the existing layer, start/end as physical left/right, or a drawer for a multi-page workflow. Related components: `Dialog`, `Popover`, `ApplicationShell`, and `FormActions`.

files-dropzone--docs.name: Docs
files-dropzone--docs.title: Files/DropZone
files-dropzone--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DropZone.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible native-file drop target with visible labelling, optional guidance and invalid feedback, MIME, extension, count and byte-size validation, accepted and rejected File callbacks, atomic file-read failure handling, and a FileTrigger fallback composition."
/>

# DropZone

`DropZone` accepts native files through drag and drop, validates them and returns successfully read files to the application.

## Import

```tsx
import { DropZone, FileTrigger } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DropZone` when dragging files from the operating system is a useful primary interaction. Always provide a keyboard- and touch-operable `FileTrigger` fallback when selection is required.

## When not to use

Use `FileTrigger` alone for a compact chooser or the `FileUpload` pattern for a complete upload workflow. `DropZone` does not upload, store, preview or remove files.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DropZone, FileTrigger } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DropZone.Root
  acceptedFileTypes={['application/pdf']}
  aria-label="Documents"
  onFiles={(files) => queueFiles(files)}
>
  <DropZone.Label>Drop documents here</DropZone.Label>
  <DropZone.Description>PDF documents only.</DropZone.Description>
  <FileTrigger.Root
    acceptedFileTypes={['application/pdf']}
    onFiles={queueFiles}
  >
    <FileTrigger.Trigger>Choose documents</FileTrigger.Trigger>
  </FileTrigger.Root>
  <DropZone.Feedback />
</DropZone.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SyntheticDrop} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                                            |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DropZone.Root`        | Owns native drop interaction, file reading, validation state and callbacks.        |
| `DropZone.Label`       | Persistent visible drop-target label.                                              |
| `DropZone.Description` | Associated guidance about interaction or accepted files.                           |
| `DropZone.Feedback`    | Announces Breeze-owned file count, type and size rejections.                       |
| `DropZone.Error`       | Application validation message, rendered as an alert only while `invalid` is true. |

Keep all parts inside `Root`. A nested `FileTrigger` is a separate public compound component and should use the same acceptance rules to avoid inconsistent results.

## Files, failures and callbacks

`acceptedFileTypes` accepts exact MIME types, wildcard groups such as `'image/*'`, and extensions such as `'.csv'`. `maxFileSize` is bytes. `allowsMultiple` defaults to `false`. `onFiles` receives the accepted native `File[]`; `onReject` receives rejected files with one or more stable reasons: `'file-count'`, `'file-size'` and `'file-type'`. Mixed valid and invalid drops deliver both accepted files and rejection records.

Reading precedes validation. If any dropped item cannot be read, the entire mixed drop is atomic: `onFileReadError` runs once with `DropZoneFileReadFailure[]`, while `onFiles` and `onReject` are not called. Each failure supplies `cause`, zero-based `itemIndex`, and browser-supplied `name` and `type`. File-reading failures are distinct from constraint rejections and `Feedback` does not render them; show application-owned recovery guidance.

`invalid` controls `Error` and accessible invalid state. `required` is presentation and validation semantics only because a drop zone has no native submitted value. `disabled` prevents focus and dropping. There is no controlled file value, read-only state or loading prop; keep selected files and upload progress in application state.

## Keyboard, accessibility and direction

- The drop target can receive focus, but dragging is not a complete keyboard interaction. Include a clearly labelled `FileTrigger.Trigger` fallback.
- Provide a concise root accessible name, such as `aria-label="Documents"`, and a persistent visible `Label`. Use `Description` for type, size and count constraints.
- `Feedback` and `Error` use alert semantics. Do not render generic error prose in `Feedback`; it is reserved for Breeze constraint rejections.
- Active drop-target appearance is supplementary. Do not rely on border colour alone to communicate acceptance, rejection or required state.
- Visible content inherits provider locale and direction. Native filenames and files are not transformed; Breeze-owned rejection text is fixed component copy, so use `onReject` when the product needs different domain or localisation handling.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not make drag and drop the only route, treat client-side constraints as security, confuse byte counts with megabytes, or call `onFiles` yourself after `onFileReadError`. Keep nested `FileTrigger` constraints aligned. Use `FileAttachment` to display a selected file and `FileUpload` for the composed workflow.
files-dropzone--docs.summary: Accessible native-file drop target with visible labelling, optional guidance and invalid feedback, MIME, extension, count and byte-size validation, accepted and rejected File callbacks, atomic file-read failure handling, and a FileTrigger fallback composition.

fields-fieldset--docs.name: Docs
fields-fieldset--docs.title: Fields/Fieldset
fields-fieldset--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Fieldset.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Native fieldset and legend composition for naming, describing, disabling, and validating a related group of form controls."
/>

# Fieldset

`Fieldset` groups related form controls with native `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` semantics plus associated description and error text.

## Import

```tsx
import { Fieldset, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when several controls answer one question or form one meaningful group, such as contact details or delivery preferences. Prefer `FormSection` for a visual form section that does not need native grouped-control semantics. `RadioGroup` and `CheckboxGroup` already provide their own accessible group naming, so do not add an extra `Fieldset` unless a larger set of distinct controls genuinely needs another group level.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Fieldset, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Fieldset.Root>
  <Fieldset.Legend>Contact details</Fieldset.Legend>
  <Fieldset.Description>
    Provide at least one contact route.
  </Fieldset.Description>
  <TextField.Root>
    <TextField.Label>Email address</TextField.Label>
    <TextField.Input name="email" type="email" />
  </TextField.Root>
</Fieldset.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                                 |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Fieldset.Root`        | Native fieldset that owns group-level disabled and invalid semantics.   |
| `Fieldset.Legend`      | Native legend and persistent accessible group name.                     |
| `Fieldset.Description` | Group-level guidance included in the fieldset’s accessible description. |
| `Fieldset.Error`       | Group-level validation feedback included while `Root` is invalid.       |

Every `Description` and `Error` must be inside its `Root`; the generated associations depend on that composition. Place `Legend` first so browser and assistive-technology behaviour is predictable.

## State and behaviour

`Fieldset` does not own values or callbacks. Descendant controls remain responsible for their individual state. Native `disabled` on `Root` disables all descendant form controls and prevents their normal form submission. `invalid` adds group-level invalid semantics and associates `Error`; it does not automatically mark each descendant invalid. Render `Error` with a recovery instruction when the group fails validation.

Loading, read-only, and required state are not group-level `Fieldset` APIs. Express them on the appropriate descendant controls, or disable the fieldset only if every control is unavailable. An empty fieldset is generally a content error, not an empty state.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

`Fieldset` adds no keyboard interaction: Tab follows the descendant controls’ natural order. The `Legend` should be concise because some screen readers announce it with each control. Do not use a heading or styled paragraph instead of `Legend`. Keep `Description` useful at the group level and use each field’s own description for control-specific formats.

Native semantics, Unicode content, and surrounding `dir` are preserved. The fieldset layout follows writing direction without requiring reordered markup.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use `Fieldset` as a generic bordered container, omit its `Legend`, nest it merely for spacing, or rely on group `invalid` to explain which descendant needs correction. Use `CheckboxGroup` for related multiple choices, `RadioGroup` for one-of-many choices, and `FormSection` for broader form organisation.
fields-fieldset--docs.summary: Native fieldset and legend composition for naming, describing, disabling, and validating a related group of form controls.

files-filetrigger--docs.name: Docs
files-filetrigger--docs.title: Files/FileTrigger
files-filetrigger--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './FileTrigger.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Keyboard-operable native file selection with MIME, extension, count and byte-size constraints; accepted and rejected File callbacks; form attributes; and built-in rejection announcements."
/>

# FileTrigger

`FileTrigger` opens the browser’s native file chooser, validates the selected native `File` objects and returns accepted files to the application.

## Import

```tsx
import { FileTrigger } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `FileTrigger` when a button should open the native file or directory chooser. Use `DropZone` when drag and drop is the primary affordance, usually with `FileTrigger` as its keyboard and touch fallback. Use the `FileUpload` pattern when you need a composed upload workflow. `FileTrigger` selects and validates files; it does not upload, persist, preview or remove them.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { FileTrigger } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<FileTrigger.Root
  acceptedFileTypes={['application/pdf']}
  maxFileSize={10 * 1024 * 1024}
  onFiles={(files) => queueFiles(files)}
  onReject={(rejections) => recordRejections(rejections)}
>
  <FileTrigger.Trigger>Choose document</FileTrigger.Trigger>
  <FileTrigger.Feedback />
</FileTrigger.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.KeyboardSelection} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                                             |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `FileTrigger.Root`     | Owns the hidden native file input, selection constraints, validation and callbacks. |
| `FileTrigger.Trigger`  | Keyboard-operable button that opens the native chooser.                             |
| `FileTrigger.Feedback` | Announces Breeze-owned file count, type and size rejection messages.                |

Both parts must be inside `Root`. `Feedback` renders nothing until a rejection occurs and then uses an alert region.

## Selection, validation and callbacks

`acceptedFileTypes` accepts exact MIME types (`'application/pdf'`), wildcard groups (`'image/*'`) and filename extensions (`'.csv'`). Matching is case-insensitive. `maxFileSize` is bytes. `allowsMultiple` defaults to `false`; extra files are rejected with reason `'file-count'`. A rejected file can contain several stable reasons: `'file-count'`, `'file-size'` and `'file-type'`.

For every completed selection, `onFiles` receives the accepted `File[]`, including an empty array when every file is rejected or the chooser is cleared. `onReject` runs only when rejections exist and receives `FileRejection[]`, where each record contains the native `file` and all `reasons`. Mixed selections deliver accepted files and rejected records separately. `Feedback` reports each rejected filename and Breeze-owned reason text; use `onReject` for analytics or application-specific handling.

`acceptDirectory` requests directory selection. `defaultCamera="user"` or `"environment"` supplies the native capture hint for compatible media inputs; browser and device behaviour varies. `name`, `form` and `required` apply to the hidden native input. The application still owns upload state and server errors. `invalid` exposes application validation but does not create an error message. There is no read-only or loading mode; use `disabled` while selection must not open.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses `Trigger`; Enter or Space opens the native chooser. The browser and operating system own navigation inside that chooser and return focus afterwards.
- Give `Trigger` a concise action label that identifies what is selected. Do not use an icon without an accessible name.
- Keep `Feedback` present when using Breeze validation so rejected files are announced. Do not rely only on native chooser filtering: validate callbacks and server input as well.
- `required`, `invalid` and `disabled` are applied to the native input for assistive technology. Empty required submission returns focus and invalid state to `Trigger`; explain application-specific invalid state in nearby text.
- Native chooser language, ordering and capture UI are browser/platform controlled. Visible labels inherit provider locale and direction, but native `File` values are not localised.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not expect `acceptedFileTypes` to be a security boundary, express `maxFileSize` in megabytes, start an upload from render, or treat chooser cancellation as an error. Do not add `onClick` to `Trigger`; `Root` owns opening behaviour. Use `DropZone` for drag and drop and `FileUpload` for a full attachment workflow.

layout-grid--docs.title: Layout/Grid
layout-grid--docs.content: import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as Stories from './Grid.stories'; # Grid `Grid` arranges related content in equal responsive columns or the canonical field-control-action track template. ## Import ```tsx import { Grid } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui'; ``` ## When to use Use `Grid` when content needs aligned rows and columns or a column count that changes at Breeze breakpoints. Use `Stack` for a single vertical flow, `Inline` for a wrapping row, and `Table` for genuinely tabular data. ## Basic example ```tsx import { Grid, Surface } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui'; export function SummaryGrid() { return ( First summary Second summary Third summary ); } ``` ## Columns, templates, and spacing `columns` accepts `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `6`, or `12`, directly or as `{ base, sm?, md?, lg? }`. Breakpoints are mobile-first at 681 px, 901 px, and 1181 px. `gap` uses the Breeze spacing values `none`, `xs`, `sm`, `control`, `compact`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xxl`, and `page` in the same scalar or responsive form. Use `template="field-control-action"` only for the established three-track form row: a flexible field, an automatic-width control, and a square final action. While a template is active, `columns` is ignored. ```tsx Status ``` ## API ## Accessibility and direction Native `div` attributes such as `id`, `aria-*`, and `data-*` are supported; inline `style` is excluded. `Grid` has no interactive, controlled, loading, invalid, empty, or error state. `Grid` is a CSS layout, not an ARIA grid, and supplies no role or keyboard interaction. Keep DOM order meaningful because responsive columns do not reorder content. Direction-aware CSS handles inline flow; avoid visual reordering that would diverge from reading and focus order. ## Common mistakes - Do not use `Grid` for data requiring row/column headers, sorting, or selection; use `Table` or `GridList`. - Do not pass both a meaningful `columns` value and `template`; the template wins. - Do not assume every item spans one conceptual column in a 12-column layout; Breeze exposes no span API. ## Related components Use `Container` around page grids, `Stack` or `Inline` for one-dimensional flow, and `Surface` or `Card` for the items.
layout-grid--docs.summary: A bounded responsive grid with stable source order and canonical track templates.

collections-gridlist--docs.name: Docs
collections-gridlist--docs.title: Collections/GridList
collections-gridlist--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './GridList.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Selectable interactive rows with stable keys, embedded controls, sections, actions, responsive grid layout, and optional virtualization."
/>

# GridList

`GridList` presents selectable or actionable rows whose content may include independently focusable controls.

```tsx
import { GridList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `GridList` for rich rows or tiles that need collection focus, typeahead, selection, row actions, or embedded controls. Use [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs) for non-interactive content, [ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs) for choosing simple options, [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs) for aligned columns, and [Card](?path=/docs/data-display-card--docs) for independent articles.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { GridList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

const records = [
  { id: 'alpha', name: 'Alpha' },
  { id: 'beta', name: 'Beta' },
];

export function RecordList() {
  return (
    <GridList.Root aria-label="Records" items={records}>
      {(record) => (
        <GridList.Item id={record.id} textValue={record.name}>
          {record.name}
        </GridList.Item>
      )}
    </GridList.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.EmbeddedControlsAndSelection} />

## Anatomy

- `GridList.Root` owns grid semantics, focus, typeahead, selection, layout, empty content, and virtualization.
- `GridList.Item` is one keyed row or tile and may expose a row-level action.
- `GridList.Section` groups statically authored items under a stable key.
- `GridList.Header` is the visible heading inside a section.
- `GridList.LoadMore` is a visible loading row and intersection-driven load-more sentinel.

## Collection content and keys

Author static `GridList.Item` children, or pass `items` and a renderer. Dynamic items must include a stable `id` whose value is a `string` or `number`. Every item repeats that stable key in `GridList.Item id`. `textValue` is required for typeahead and an accessible plain-text representation, especially when visible content is structured.

Selection values are `Array<string | number>` or the literal `'all'`. Do not use array positions, translated labels, or transient object identities as keys.

## Selection and actions

Uncontrolled selection uses `defaultSelection` and may observe changes with `onSelectionChange`. Controlled selection requires both `selection` and `onSelectionChange`. Read-only selection requires `selection`, `readOnly`, and no callback. The initial uncontrolled selection is empty.

`multiple` defaults to `false`. `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next selection, not only the changed key. `GridList.Item onAction` receives that item's stable key. Keep embedded buttons and links as real controls; their activation remains separate from row selection and action.

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import {
  GridList,
  type CollectionSelection,
} from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ControlledRecords() {
  const [selection, setSelection] = useState<CollectionSelection>(['alpha']);

  return (
    <GridList.Root
      aria-label="Records"
      onSelectionChange={setSelection}
      selection={selection}
    >
      <GridList.Item id="alpha" textValue="Alpha">
        Alpha
      </GridList.Item>
      <GridList.Item id="beta" textValue="Beta">
        Beta
      </GridList.Item>
    </GridList.Root>
  );
}
```

## Layout, empty, disabled, loading, and error states

`layout="stack"` is the default. `layout="grid"` creates auto-fitting tiles while preserving grid-list semantics. `orientation` defaults to `vertical` and sets the primary arrow-key axis; use `horizontal` only when the visual arrangement and expected navigation agree. `disabledKeys` disables matching keys globally; `GridList.Item disabled` disables one item. Disabled rows cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.

`emptyContent` defaults to `No items`; supply translated application copy. `GridList.LoadMore` calls `onLoadMore` when its sentinel approaches the viewport. Set `loading` while the returned request is pending so duplicate requests are suppressed. `offset` defaults to `1` viewport proportion. The collection has no built-in error presentation: replace or accompany it with application-owned feedback and offer a retry action.

## Virtualization

Pass `virtualization` only for collections large enough to justify windowing. Both modes require `viewportHeight` in pixels.

| Mode                   | Required                      | Optional defaults and behaviour                                    |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `{ mode: 'fixed' }`    | `rowHeight`, `viewportHeight` | Uniform authoritative row height; `gap` defaults to `0`.           |
| `{ mode: 'variable' }` | `viewportHeight`              | `estimatedRowHeight` defaults to `48`; rendered rows are measured. |

Both accept `overscan` (default `0` beyond the directional buffer), `loadingRowHeight` (default `48`), and `estimatedSectionHeight`. Grid-list virtualization also accepts `minimumItemWidth` (default `224`) and `maximumColumns` (defaults to available width). Keep keys stable and provide realistic estimates; do not virtualize merely to hide expensive row rendering.

<Canvas of={Stories.VariableVirtualizationAndLoading} />

## Keyboard and accessibility

Give every root a visible label or `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`. Arrow keys move row focus along `orientation`; Home and End move to collection boundaries, typeahead uses `textValue`, and standard selection keys follow the current single or multiple selection mode. Enter or equivalent activation invokes an actionable row. Tab moves into and out of embedded controls without changing their semantics. Exact platform selection modifiers follow React Aria conventions.

Translate labels, empty/loading copy, section headings, and `textValue`. Logical layout and focus movement account for the inherited direction; do not reverse source order for RTL.

Parts support relevant native `div` attributes, `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and typed refs. Click, value, children, and inline-style props owned by the collection API are intentionally excluded where applicable.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not omit `textValue` or use unstable keys.
- Do not mix `selection` with `defaultSelection`, or mark uncontrolled state read-only.
- Do not attach click handlers to row wrappers; use `onAction` or an embedded control.
- Do not leave `loading` false while an asynchronous load-more request is pending.
- Do not use `GridList` for plain prose or tabular comparisons.

## Related components

[ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs), [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs), [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs), and [Card](?path=/docs/data-display-card--docs).
collections-gridlist--docs.summary: Selectable interactive rows with stable keys, embedded controls, sections, actions, responsive grid layout, and optional virtualization.

actions-iconbutton--docs.name: Docs
actions-iconbutton--docs.title: Actions/IconButton
actions-iconbutton--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './IconButton.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A compact icon-only action with a mandatory accessible label."
/>

# IconButton

`IconButton` performs an application action in a square icon-only control whose required `aria-label` supplies its accessible name.

## Import

```tsx
import { IconButton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { SettingsIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';
```

## When to use

Use `IconButton` for a familiar, repeated action where space is constrained and a text label would add noise. Use `Button` when visible text improves recognition, `Link` for navigation, or `Tooltip` to provide supplementary explanation without replacing the accessible name.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { IconButton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { SettingsIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';

export function SettingsButton() {
  return (
    <IconButton aria-label="Open settings" onAction={() => openSettings()}>
      <SettingsIcon />
    </IconButton>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Activation} />

## Label, icon, and emphasis

`aria-label` is required and should name the action, not describe the glyph: use “Delete item”, not “Bin icon”. The child icon is always hidden from assistive technology because the button owns the name. Keep one clear icon as the child.

Appearances are `solid`, `subtle`, `outline`, and `ghost`; variants are `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, and `dark`; square sizes are `sm`, `md`, and `lg`. The defaults deliberately produce a medium, secondary ghost action.

## Activation, forms, and state

`onAction` fires once for pointer, touch, or keyboard activation. `type` is limited to `button` and `submit` and defaults to `button`. `disabled` prevents interaction. `loading` prevents activation while preserving focusability, sets `aria-busy`, retains the accessible label, and temporarily displays a decorative spinner. Error reporting belongs to the surrounding workflow; there is no invalid, read-only, controlled, or empty state.

<Canvas of={Stories.States} />

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility and keyboard behaviour

Relevant native button attributes such as `name`, `value`, `form`, `autoFocus`, `aria-*`, and `data-*` are supported. Breeze owns native click, disabled, pressed, busy, type, class, and style behaviour.

`Enter` and `Space` activate enabled icon buttons, and keyboard focus has a visible indicator. Do not depend on a tooltip as the only name, and do not repeat the icon name in the label. Translate `aria-label` with the rest of the interface. Verify that the chosen icon remains understandable in both directions; directional icon mirroring is icon-specific.

## Common mistakes

- Do not omit `aria-label` or use a vague label such as “Click here”.
- Do not place visible text inside an `IconButton`; use `Button`.
- Do not use an icon-only control for an unfamiliar or high-consequence action without sufficient nearby context.
- Do not add `aria-label` to the child icon; it is deliberately decorative here.

## Related components

Use `Button` for visible labels, `ToggleButton` for persistent on/off state, `Tooltip` for supplementary help, and the curated Breeze icon catalogue for supported icons.

foundation-icontile--docs.title: Foundation/IconTile
foundation-icontile--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './IconTile.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Non-interactive semantic presentation tile for curated icons and short visual markers."
/>

# IconTile

`IconTile` places an icon or short marker in canonical geometry with constrained semantic colour emphasis.

## Import

```tsx
import { IconTile } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { CalendarIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';
```

## When to use

- Use as a supporting visual marker in a header, state panel, summary, or record.
- Use `variant` to reinforce a meaning already expressed in text.
- Use `decorative={false}` only when the marker contributes information not available in nearby text.

## When not to use

- Use `IconButton` when the icon activates an action; IconTile is not interactive.
- Use `Avatar` when the content represents a named person or entity.
- Use `Badge` for compact visible status text.
- Use a curated icon directly when no enclosing geometry or semantic surface is needed.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { IconTile, Inline, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { CalendarIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';

export function ScheduleSummary() {
  return (
    <Inline align="center" gap="sm" wrap={false}>
      <IconTile variant="info">
        <CalendarIcon />
      </IconTile>
      <Typography>Next review date</Typography>
    </Inline>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SemanticTiles} />

## Accessibility mode

`decorative` defaults to `true`, adding `aria-hidden="true"` to the whole tile. This is correct when adjacent visible text supplies the meaning. Any accessible label on a nested icon is also hidden by the tile.

When the marker is the only source of information, expose it and name the meaningful child:

```tsx
import { IconTile } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { WarningIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';

export function WarningMarker() {
  return (
    <IconTile decorative={false} variant="warning">
      <WarningIcon aria-label="Needs attention" />
    </IconTile>
  );
}
```

For visible short text such as a status code, `decorative={false}` exposes that text naturally. Do not expose both a marker and adjacent text with the same wording if that creates repetitive announcements.

## Variants, geometry, and size

Variants are `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, `dark`, and `neutral`. They set the border, background, and foreground together. Semantic variants reinforce, but never replace, visible status language.

`square` is the default geometry; `circle` is useful for directional or compact status markers. Sizes are `sm` (36 pixels), `md` (40 pixels, the default), and `lg` (56 pixels). Child icons sized in `em` inherit the tile's text scale.

`bordered={false}` removes the ordinary semantic border. Breeze may still expose a boundary in forced-colour mode so the marker remains perceivable.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## State and behaviour

Relevant native span attributes such as `id`, `aria-label` when exposed, `data-*`, and relationship attributes are forwarded. `aria-hidden` is owned by `decorative`; arbitrary inline `style` is not a public styling contract.

IconTile has no keyboard behaviour and no controlled, uncontrolled, disabled, loading, invalid, error, empty, or read-only state. It must not receive action semantics or click handling. State belongs to the surrounding component; the tile only presents a marker.

The geometry is direction-neutral. Directional icons retain their named glyph orientation; select the icon that describes the intended concept in the current interface. Child text follows provider direction but is not translated or formatted.

## Common mistakes and limitations

- Do not use colour or an icon alone to communicate critical state.
- Do not set `decorative={false}` without accessible meaning in the child or wrapper.
- Do not give a nested icon an `aria-label` while leaving the tile decorative.
- Do not put long text, controls, or multiple competing icons inside the tile.
- Use typed props rather than overriding canonical geometry or semantic colours with classes.

## Related components

- Use the curated `icons` subpath for the marker glyph.
- Use `IconButton` for an icon-only action.
- Use `Avatar` for identity and `Badge` for status text.
- Use `StatePanel` when the marker belongs to a complete empty, loading, or error composition.
foundation-icontile--docs.summary: Non-interactive semantic presentation tile for curated icons and short visual markers.

layout-inline--docs.name: Docs
layout-inline--docs.title: Layout/Inline
layout-inline--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Inline.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A horizontal layout with responsive spacing, alignment, distribution, and wrapping."
/>

# Inline

`Inline` arranges related children along the inline axis and wraps them by default.

## Import

```tsx
import { Inline } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `Inline` for button rows, metadata, badges, and controls that form one horizontal group. Use `Toolbar` when related controls need toolbar semantics and arrow-key navigation, `Stack` for vertical flow, or `Grid` for aligned columns.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Button, Inline } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function Actions() {
  return (
    <Inline gap="compact" justify="end">
      <Button appearance="outline">Cancel</Button>
      <Button>Save changes</Button>
    </Inline>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Wrapping} />

## Layout and responsive spacing

`align` controls the cross-axis and includes `baseline` for text with different sizes. `justify` distributes items along the row. Leave `wrap` enabled unless the composition is guaranteed to fit at every supported viewport and zoom level.

`gap` accepts `none`, `xs`, `sm`, `control`, `compact`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xxl`, or `page`, directly or as `{ base, sm?, md?, lg? }`; breakpoints start at 681 px, 901 px, and 1181 px.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility and direction

Relevant native `div` attributes are supported, including `id`, `aria-*`, and `data-*`; inline `style` is excluded. There are no controlled, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error states.

`Inline` adds no role, label, focus management, or keyboard behaviour. Wrapping changes visual rows without changing reading or tab order. In right-to-left direction, the browser lays out the inline axis accordingly; do not hard-code left/right spacing or reverse DOM order.

## Common mistakes

- Do not set `wrap={false}` for user-generated labels or action sets that may grow after translation.
- Do not use spacing characters or per-child margins instead of `gap`.
- Do not use `Inline` as a substitute for `Toolbar` semantics.

## Related components

Use `Stack` for vertical flow, `Grid` for responsive columns, `Toolbar` for keyboard-navigable actions, and `ButtonGroup` for a canonical grouped-action pattern.
layout-inline--docs.summary: A horizontal layout with responsive spacing, alignment, distribution, and wrapping.

fields-inputgroup--docs.name: Docs
fields-inputgroup--docs.title: Fields/InputGroup
fields-inputgroup--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './InputGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Visual single-surface composition for a Breeze field control with non-interactive prefixes, suffixes, units, or a closely related action."
/>

# InputGroup

`InputGroup` visually joins a field control with non-interactive addons or a directly related action without taking ownership of field state.

## Import

```tsx
import { InputGroup, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for a protocol prefix, unit suffix, currency marker, or a compact action that operates on the adjacent field. Prefer a field’s own group part (`SearchField.Group`, `NumberField.Group`, or `ComboBox.Group`) when that component supplies one. Do not use it merely to lay out unrelated controls; use `Inline`, `Stack`, or `FormActions` instead.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { InputGroup, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<TextField.Root>
  <TextField.Label>Budget</TextField.Label>
  <InputGroup.Root>
    <InputGroup.Addon aria-hidden="true">£</InputGroup.Addon>
    <TextField.Input name="budget" inputMode="decimal" />
    <InputGroup.Addon>GBP</InputGroup.Addon>
  </InputGroup.Root>
</TextField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.PrefixAndSuffix} />

## Anatomy

| Part               | Purpose                                                                |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `InputGroup.Root`  | Creates one visual perimeter around ordered controls and addons.       |
| `InputGroup.Addon` | Renders a non-interactive prefix or suffix such as a unit or protocol. |

`InputGroup` is visual composition, not field semantics. Keep it inside the owning field root so the field label, disabled state, and invalid state continue to reach the actual control. Author children in logical reading order; the component adapts its borders and spacing for right-to-left layouts.

## Semantics and state

`Root` defaults to `role="presentation"`. Use `role="group"` or `role="region"` only when the composition itself needs an accessible boundary, and then provide `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. A visual currency symbol that repeats information available in the label or description should be `aria-hidden`; essential units should remain available to assistive technology.

State comes from child controls and their owning field root. `InputGroup` has no value, callback, disabled, read-only, invalid, loading, empty, or error API. Breeze reflects descendant focus, disabled, and invalid data states on the shared perimeter where supported. Do not implement application state on the group.

Match `Addon` size to the adjacent control: `sm`, `md` (default), or `lg`. Mixed sizes create broken alignment and misleading hit areas.

## Keyboard and accessibility

`InputGroup` adds no keyboard behaviour. Focus follows its interactive descendants in DOM order. `Addon` is not interactive and must not contain buttons or links; place a real Breeze button directly in `Root` for a related action. Ensure the field’s visible label names the editable control, and ensure an adjacent icon-only action has its own accessible name.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not add a second accessible label to a presentational group, put interactive content inside `Addon`, mix control sizes, or use CSS to rearrange the authored reading order. Use `SearchField.Group`, `NumberField.Group`, or `ComboBox.Group` for their native compound anatomy; use layout primitives for unrelated controls.
fields-inputgroup--docs.summary: Visual single-surface composition for a Breeze field control with non-interactive prefixes, suffixes, units, or a closely related action.
