actions-linkbutton--docs.name: Docs
actions-linkbutton--docs.title: Actions/LinkButton
actions-linkbutton--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './LinkButton.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Router-neutral navigation presented with canonical button emphasis."
/>

# LinkButton

`LinkButton` navigates to a destination while using the visual hierarchy of a button.

## Import

```tsx
import { LinkButton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `LinkButton` when navigation is the outcome but the destination must be prominent, such as “Continue” or “Create item”. Use `Button` for application actions and form submission, and `Link` for inline or lower-emphasis navigation.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { LinkButton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ContinueLink() {
  return <LinkButton href="/next-step">Continue</LinkButton>;
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Internal} />

## Routing and native navigation

Every `LinkButton` requires a real `href`, preserving native link semantics and fallback navigation. When `BreezeProvider` has a router adapter, an unmodified primary click on a root-relative URL such as `/items` calls `router.navigate(href)`. Protocol-relative URLs, external URLs, downloads, non-`_self` targets, and clicks with modifier keys retain native browser behaviour.

```tsx
<LinkButton href="/reports/latest" download>
  Download report
</LinkButton>
```

## Emphasis and state

Appearances are `solid`, `subtle`, `outline`, and `ghost`; semantic variants are `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, and `dark`; sizes are `sm`, `md`, and `lg`. Choose a danger variant only when the destination itself represents a destructive or high-risk path, not for an action that should be a `Button`.

`disabled` prevents navigation, removes the `href`, and exposes `aria-disabled` while retaining the link’s accessible identity. Prefer removing unavailable navigation or explaining why it is unavailable. `LinkButton` has no loading, callback, controlled, read-only, invalid, error, or empty state.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility, keyboard, and internationalisation

Relevant native anchor attributes such as `rel`, `hreflang`, `referrerPolicy`, `aria-*`, and `data-*` are supported. Breeze owns click interception, `href`, `download`, `className`, and inline styling.

Use destination-oriented text that makes sense out of context. `Enter` follows an enabled link; `Space` does not emulate a button. For a new browsing context, communicate that behaviour and use an appropriate `rel`. Decorative icons must not replace the visible link purpose. Allow translated labels to grow and ensure direction-sensitive icons are chosen or mirrored by the icon implementation where appropriate.

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `LinkButton` for mutations, modal triggers, or submit behaviour.
- Do not omit `href` and simulate navigation in an action callback.
- Do not intercept external, download, target, or modified-click behaviour in application code.
- Do not use `disabled` as a long-term substitute for clear navigation rules.

## Related components

Use `Link` for ordinary navigation, `Button` for actions, `IconButton` for icon-only actions, and configure routing through `BreezeProvider`.
actions-linkbutton--docs.summary: Router-neutral navigation presented with canonical button emphasis.

data-display-list--docs.name: Docs
data-display-list--docs.title: Data Display/List
data-display-list--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './List.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Native ordered or unordered content list with deliberately non-interactive semantics."
/>

# List

`List` presents related text or content using native ordered or unordered list semantics.

```tsx
import { List } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `List` for prose-like groups where order either does or does not matter.

## When not to use

Use [DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs) for term-value metadata, [ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs) for option selection, or [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs) for selectable rows with richer content.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { List } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ReviewSteps() {
  return (
    <List.Root ordered>
      <List.Item>Prepare the changes</List.Item>
      <List.Item>Review the summary</List.Item>
      <List.Item>Publish the release</List.Item>
    </List.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.OrderedAndUnordered} />

## Anatomy and semantics

- `List.Root` renders `ul` by default and `ol` when `ordered` is `true`.
- `List.Item` renders one native `li`.

List owns no selection, action, focus-management, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error behaviour.

## Accessibility and keyboard behaviour

Use `List.Item` children directly inside `List.Root` so native list semantics remain intact. The component has no special keyboard interaction. Native list semantics work in both directions; translate visible content and choose `ordered` only when sequence is meaningful.

Both parts support the relevant native list attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; inline `style` is intentionally excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `ordered` only to obtain number markers when order is irrelevant.
- Do not use list items as buttons without rendering a real interactive control inside them.
- Do not use `List` as a selectable collection; it deliberately has no collection value format or callbacks.

## Related components

[DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs), [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs), and [NavigationList](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-navigationlist--docs) cover metadata, interactive rows, and navigation respectively.
data-display-list--docs.summary: Native ordered or unordered content list with deliberately non-interactive semantics.

collections-listbox--docs.name: Docs
collections-listbox--docs.title: Collections/ListBox
collections-listbox--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './ListBox.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Persistent selectable collection with static or typed items, single or multiple selection, disabled keys, actions, empty state, incremental loading, and fixed or variable virtualization."
/>

# ListBox

`ListBox` presents a persistent collection of options with keyboard focus, typeahead, and optional single or multiple selection.

## Import

```tsx
import { ListBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when options should remain visible, when multiple selection is needed, or when a large collection benefits from virtualisation or incremental loading. Prefer `Select` for compact single selection, `ComboBox` for editable filtering, `RadioGroup` for a short visible one-of-many choice, and `List` for non-interactive content. Do not use listbox options as navigation links or a collection of unrelated buttons.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { ListBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<ListBox.Root aria-label="Priority" defaultSelection={['normal']}>
  <ListBox.Item id="low" textValue="Low">
    Low
  </ListBox.Item>
  <ListBox.Item id="normal" textValue="Normal">
    Normal
  </ListBox.Item>
  <ListBox.Item id="high" textValue="High">
    High
  </ListBox.Item>
</ListBox.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part               | Purpose                                                                                                           |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ListBox.Root`     | Owns collection rendering, focus, typeahead, selection, empty state, orientation, and optional virtualisation.    |
| `ListBox.Item`     | One selectable option with a stable key, plain-text representation, disabled state, and optional semantic action. |
| `ListBox.LoadMore` | Loading row and deduplicated intersection sentinel for application-owned incremental loading.                     |

`Root` must have an accessible name through `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Use `onAction` for an option’s semantic action only when that action is consistent with listbox interaction; it receives the item key.

## Static and dynamic collections

Static collections use authored `Item` children. Dynamic collections require items with stable `id: string | number` and a renderer.

```tsx
<ListBox.Root items={people} aria-label="Assignees" multiple>
  {(person) => (
    <ListBox.Item id={person.id} textValue={person.name}>
      {person.name}
    </ListBox.Item>
  )}
</ListBox.Root>
```

Never mix static children with `items`. `textValue` is required for typeahead and accessibility, especially when visible content contains markup. `disabledKeys` disables matching keys without changing the data. Item-level `disabled` is useful for static collections.

## Selection and state

Selections use `CollectionKey[] | 'all'`, where each key is a string or number. Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next selection. `multiple` defaults to `false`; even single selection is represented as an array.

`required` adds required semantics; `invalid` adds invalid semantics and styling, but validation messages belong in surrounding form composition. `readOnly` exposes immutable semantics and prevents application state changes. An empty collection displays `emptyContent`, defaulting to `'No options'`. The component has no root loading prop; use `LoadMore` or application-owned status content.

`orientation` defaults to `vertical`; horizontal listboxes should contain a small, naturally horizontal set.

## Virtualisation and loading

Pass `virtualization` only for collections large enough to justify windowing. Fixed mode requires an authoritative `rowHeight`; variable mode measures live rows and uses `estimatedRowHeight` only initially.

```tsx
<ListBox.Root
  items={items}
  aria-label="Results"
  virtualization={{ mode: 'fixed', viewportHeight: 320, rowHeight: 48, gap: 4 }}
>
  {(item) => (
    <ListBox.Item id={item.id} textValue={item.name}>
      {item.name}
    </ListBox.Item>
  )}
</ListBox.Root>
```

Common virtualisation options are `viewportHeight` (required pixels), `gap` (`0`), `overscan` (`0` beyond the engine buffer), `loadingRowHeight` (`48`), and `estimatedSectionHeight`. Variable mode’s `estimatedRowHeight` defaults to `48`.

`LoadMore` calls `onLoadMore` once when its offset threshold is reached and suppresses duplicate calls while `loading` is true. `offset` defaults to `1` viewport proportion. The application owns fetching, errors, retries, end-of-list state, and stable item accumulation.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab focuses the listbox; arrow keys move the active option along its orientation.
- Typeahead matches `textValue`. Enter or Space selects according to selection mode; selection behaviour follows accessible listbox conventions.
- Disabled options cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.
- Give the root a persistent accessible name. Do not place interactive descendants inside an option.
- Right-to-left horizontal navigation follows platform direction; keys remain stable while labels and empty/loading content may be translated.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use array indexes as keys, omit the accessible root name, mix links or buttons into options, enable virtualisation without a fixed viewport height, or forget to set `loading` while a load-more request is pending. Use `Select`, `ComboBox`, `RadioGroup`, or non-interactive `List` when their semantics fit better.
collections-listbox--docs.summary: Persistent selectable collection with static or typed items, single or multiple selection, disabled keys, actions, empty state, incremental loading, and fixed or variable virtualization.

foundation-logo--docs.name: Docs
foundation-logo--docs.title: Foundation/Logo
foundation-logo--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Logo.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Canonical accessible Motech Development brand mark in three supported sizes."
/>

# Logo

`Logo` renders the canonical Motech Development mark with a fixed accessible name and Breeze-owned brand colour.

## Import

```tsx
import { Logo } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

- Use when the Motech Development brand mark is required in application chrome or an identity treatment.
- Pair it with application-owned product text when a product name must appear beside the mark.
- Choose the nearest canonical size and let the containing layout provide spacing.

## When not to use

- Use `Avatar` for a person or entity identity; Logo is not a generic image placeholder.
- Use a curated icon for an action, status, or navigation affordance.
- Do not use Logo to represent an application, organisation, or partner that has a different identity.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Inline, Logo, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ProductIdentity() {
  return (
    <Inline align="center" gap="sm" wrap={false}>
      <Logo />
      <Typography as="span" level="label">
        Workspace
      </Typography>
    </Inline>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Sizes} />

## Anatomy and sizing

Logo is one non-interactive `span` with `role="img"` and the accessible name “Motech Development”. Its inline SVG is hidden from assistive technology so the mark is announced once.

The `sm`, `md`, and `lg` sizes set canonical surrounding text scales of 1rem, 1.25rem, and 1.5rem. The mark itself is sized in `em`, so it remains aligned with adjacent text. `md` is the default.

The mark uses the Breeze `--breeze-brand-mark` token. On an inverse Breeze surface that token supplies the approved contrasting treatment; applications should not recolour the internal SVG.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility and behaviour

Relevant native span attributes such as `id`, `data-*`, and relationship attributes are forwarded. `children`, `aria-label`, `style`, and colour overrides are intentionally excluded: the geometry, accessible brand name, and colour treatment are canonical.

Logo has no keyboard interaction and no controlled, uncontrolled, disabled, loading, invalid, or read-only state. When Logo is the only content of a link, its fixed accessible name becomes the link name; ensure “Motech Development” accurately describes that destination. If a product-home link needs a different name, include visible or visually hidden product text in the link.

The mark is direction-neutral and its accessible name is not translated by `BreezeProvider`. Product text beside it follows the provider locale and direction normally.

## Common mistakes and limitations

- Do not copy the internal SVG or depend on its paths, view box, or classes.
- Do not pass children or replace the accessible name.
- Do not stretch the mark with arbitrary width and height styles.
- Do not use Logo as a clickable element; wrap it in `Link` when it is genuinely a destination.
- The component provides only the Motech Development mark, not product lock-ups or application-owned wording.

## Related components

- Use `Avatar` for people and entity markers.
- Use `IconTile` to place an icon or short marker in semantic geometry.
- Use `Link` when the brand mark leads to a destination.
- Use `ApplicationShell` for the surrounding application identity and navigation structure.
foundation-logo--docs.summary: Canonical accessible Motech Development brand mark in three supported sizes.

primitives-navigation-menu--docs.name: Docs
primitives-navigation-menu--docs.title: Primitives/Navigation/Menu
primitives-navigation-menu--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Menu.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A transient keyboard-navigable menu for keyed actions, optional selection, destinations, and nested submenus."
/>

# Menu

`Menu` reveals a compact collection of actions or destinations from a trigger.

## Import

```tsx
import { Menu } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for secondary actions that need not remain visible.

## When not to use

Use `Select` for choosing a form value, `NavigationList` for persistent routes, `Popover` for interactive form content, and visible buttons when an action is primary or time-critical.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Menu } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Menu.Root>
  <Menu.Trigger>Actions</Menu.Trigger>
  <Menu.Popover>
    <Menu.List aria-label="Actions">
      <Menu.Item id="rename" textValue="Rename" onAction={rename}>
        Rename
      </Menu.Item>
      <Menu.Item id="archive" textValue="Archive" onAction={archive}>
        Archive
      </Menu.Item>
    </Menu.List>
  </Menu.Popover>
</Menu.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticNestedKeyboardAndTouch} />

## Anatomy

| Part           | Purpose                                               |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `Menu.Root`    | Coordinates open state, trigger focus, and dismissal. |
| `Menu.Trigger` | Button that opens the root menu.                      |
| `Menu.Popover` | Positions and portals a root or nested menu.          |
| `Menu.List`    | Keyboard-navigable static or generic item collection. |
| `Menu.Item`    | One keyed action, selectable item, or destination.    |
| `Menu.Submenu` | Pairs a trigger `Item` with a nested `Popover`.       |

`Submenu` requires exactly two children in order: its trigger `Menu.Item`, then a `Menu.Popover` containing another labelled `Menu.List`.

## Collections, selection, and callbacks

Author `Item` children directly or pass `items`, where every item has `id: string | number`, and render an item from the child function. `textValue` is required for typeahead and accessible representation.

```tsx
<Menu.List items={actions} aria-label="Actions">
  {(action) => (
    <Menu.Item id={action.id} textValue={action.label} onAction={action.run}>
      {action.label}
    </Menu.Item>
  )}
</Menu.List>
```

Selection is off unless `selection` or `defaultSelection` is supplied. Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. Values are `(string | number)[] | 'all'`; `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next value. Set `multiple` for multiple selection. `onAction` receives the activated item's id even when it is also selectable.

Root open state follows the same pattern with `defaultOpen`, or `open` plus `onOpenChange`; `open` plus `readOnly` is immutable. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean. `disabledKeys` disables matching ids and `Item.disabled` disables one item. Empty content defaults to `No actions`. Loading and load errors are application-owned and should be expressed as deliberate non-action content or adjacent feedback.

## Routing and presentation

An item without `href` is an action. A local `/path` item uses the provider router; other URLs keep native navigation. Do not combine a destination with an unrelated action. `Popover.placement` is `'bottom start'` by default and also accepts `'bottom end'`, `'top start'`, or `'top end'`; `nonModal` defaults to `false`.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Relevant button, menu, and item `aria-*` and `data-*` attributes are supported. Breeze intentionally owns click, selection, disabled, and inline style behaviour.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

Enter, Space, or Arrow Down opens the menu. Arrow Up and Arrow Down move focus; Home and End reach boundaries; character keys use `textValue`; Enter or Space activates; Escape closes and restores focus. Arrow Right enters a submenu and Arrow Left returns in left-to-right contexts; React Aria mirrors directional behaviour for RTL. Disabled items are skipped.

Label every `List`, including nested lists. Keep items concise and non-interactive inside; do not place controls in an item. Translate labels, `textValue`, and `emptyContent` together while keeping stable ids. Common mistakes are using Menu as a select field, omitting `textValue`, mixing controlled and uncontrolled props, and nesting a submenu without its own label.

Related components: `Popover`, `Select`, `NavigationList`, `Toolbar`, and `UserMenu`.
primitives-navigation-menu--docs.summary: A transient keyboard-navigable menu for keyed actions, optional selection, destinations, and nested submenus.

feedback-meter--docs.name: Docs
feedback-meter--docs.title: Feedback/Meter
feedback-meter--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Meter.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled scalar measurement within a known finite range, with clamped visual and accessible output."
/>

# Meter

`Meter` displays one scalar measurement within a known range.

```tsx
import { Meter } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a meter for a stable quantity such as storage use, signal quality, or capacity. Use [ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs) for operation completion, [MetricCard](?path=/docs/patterns-data-metriccard--docs) for a prominent standalone metric, and [Slider](?path=/docs/selection-slider--docs) when the user changes the value.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Meter } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function StorageUsage() {
  return (
    <Meter
      label="Storage used"
      value={72}
      valueText="72 gigabytes used"
      variant="warning"
    />
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Usage} />

## Range and value behaviour

`minimum` defaults to `0` and `maximum` to `100`. Both bounds must be finite and `maximum` must be greater than or equal to `minimum`; invalid bounds throw `RangeError`. Finite values outside the range are clamped consistently for visual width, visible copy, and accessible value. Use `valueText` to provide translated units or qualitative meaning.

Meter is always determinate and read-only. It has no callback, keyboard interaction, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error state.

## Variants and accessibility

`variant` defaults to `primary` and accepts `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, or `dark`. Choose a semantic colour only when it represents the measurement's meaning, and never depend on colour alone. The required visible `label` names the `meter`. Localise `label` and `valueText`; numeric formatting is application-owned. Logical layout follows the inherited direction.

<Canvas of={Stories.CustomRange} />

`Meter` supports relevant native `div` attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; `children`, `role`, and inline `style` are intentionally owned or excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `Meter` as an input or progress indicator.
- Do not pass non-finite bounds or reverse the range.
- Do not rely on an unlocalised raw number when units matter.
- Do not communicate warning or danger only through `variant`.

## Related components

[ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs), [Slider](?path=/docs/selection-slider--docs), and [MetricCard](?path=/docs/patterns-data-metriccard--docs).

primitives-navigation-navigationlist--do.name: Docs
primitives-navigation-navigationlist--do.title: Primitives/Navigation/NavigationList
primitives-navigation-navigationlist--do.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './NavigationList.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A labelled persistent navigation landmark with keyed destinations, current-page state, and horizontal or vertical layout."
/>

# NavigationList

`NavigationList` presents a persistent set of application destinations and identifies the current page.

## Import

```tsx
import { NavigationList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for side navigation or a compact horizontal destination list. Use `Menu` for transient actions, `Tabs` for views of the same resource, and `Breadcrumbs` for hierarchical orientation.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { NavigationList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<NavigationList.Root aria-label="Settings">
  <NavigationList.Item current href="/settings" id="general">
    General
  </NavigationList.Item>
  <NavigationList.Item href="/settings/security" id="security">
    Security
  </NavigationList.Item>
</NavigationList.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StatesAndOrientations} />

## Anatomy, state, and routing

| Part                  | Purpose                                                     |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `NavigationList.Root` | Renders a labelled navigation landmark containing the list. |
| `NavigationList.Item` | Renders one keyed router-neutral destination.               |

Set `current` on exactly one destination for `aria-current="page"`. `onAction` receives that item's complete `string | number` id after semantic activation; it receives no DOM event. A disabled item neither navigates nor calls `onAction`.

Local `/path` destinations use the `BreezeProvider` router adapter when present. Without an adapter, and for external URLs, normal anchors are used. There is no selection controller: route state owns `current`. The component has no loading, invalid, read-only, empty, or error presentation; render an appropriate loading or state component instead of an empty landmark.

## Orientation

`vertical` is the default and suits side navigation. `horizontal` wraps at narrow widths and suits a short set. Layout follows logical direction, while destination ids and URLs do not change during localisation.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Relevant `nav` and list-item `aria-*` and `data-*` attributes are supported. Inline styling and native click handlers are excluded from the public interaction contract.

## Accessibility and common mistakes

Every root needs a distinct accessible name when a page has more than one navigation landmark. Tab visits enabled links and Enter activates them. Use visible, translated link text, allow it to wrap, and do not rely on the current background colour alone. Do not use `onAction` as a substitute for navigation, put buttons inside items, mark several pages current, or use unstable ids.

Related components: `Breadcrumbs` for hierarchy, `Tabs` for peer panels, `Menu` for transient commands, and `NavigationSection` for a composed navigation pattern.
primitives-navigation-navigationlist--do.summary: A labelled persistent navigation landmark with keyed destinations, current-page state, and horizontal or vertical layout.

fields-numberfield--docs.name: Docs
fields-numberfield--docs.title: Fields/NumberField
fields-numberfield--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './NumberField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Locale-aware numeric entry with optional increment and decrement controls, explicit constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and validation states."
/>

# NumberField

`NumberField` collects a numeric value with locale-aware display, keyboard stepping, and optional increment and decrement buttons.

## Import

```tsx
import { NumberField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for quantities, percentages, rates, and other values that are mathematically numeric.

## When not to use

Prefer `TextField` with an appropriate `inputMode` for digit-shaped identifiers such as account numbers, telephone numbers, and postcodes. Prefer `Slider` when approximate adjustment within a bounded range is more important than exact entry.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { NumberField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<NumberField.Root defaultValue={2} min={1} max={20} step={1}>
  <NumberField.Label>Quantity</NumberField.Label>
  <NumberField.Group>
    <NumberField.DecrementButton aria-label="Decrease quantity">
      −
    </NumberField.DecrementButton>
    <NumberField.Input name="quantity" />
    <NumberField.IncrementButton aria-label="Increase quantity">
      +
    </NumberField.IncrementButton>
  </NumberField.Group>
  <NumberField.Description>Choose from 1 to 20.</NumberField.Description>
</NumberField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                          | Purpose                                                       |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `NumberField.Root`            | Owns numeric value, range, step, formatting, and field state. |
| `NumberField.Label`           | Persistently names the spinbutton.                            |
| `NumberField.Group`           | Visually joins input and optional step controls.              |
| `NumberField.Input`           | Editable locale-aware spinbutton.                             |
| `NumberField.DecrementButton` | Subtracts one configured step.                                |
| `NumberField.IncrementButton` | Adds one configured step.                                     |
| `NumberField.Description`     | Associates range or format guidance.                          |
| `NumberField.Error`           | Associates validation feedback while invalid.                 |

Stepper buttons are optional. When present, give each a literal accessible `aria-label` and match its `size` to the input.

## Numeric value and callbacks

The public value is `number | null`; `null` represents an empty input. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the parsed next number or `null`, never a DOM event and never `NaN`.

`min`, `max`, and `step` constrain stepping and validity; `step` defaults to `1`. `formatOptions` accepts `Intl.NumberFormatOptions`, for example `{ style: 'percent' }` or `{ style: 'currency', currency: 'GBP' }`. Store the numeric value, not the formatted display string.

`disabled` prevents focus, editing, and stepping. `readOnly` keeps a controlled value immutable. Set `invalid` and render `Error` for application validation. Empty input is supported through `null`. The application owns loading and asynchronous validation.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the spinbutton. Up Arrow increments and Down Arrow decrements by `step`.
- Page Up and Page Down perform larger steps where supported; Home and End move to finite bounds where supported.
- Typing, deletion, and locale-appropriate decimal and grouping characters use platform and locale behaviour.
- Always provide `Label`. Describe range, unit, or special formatting in `Description`; do not put essential unit information only in a visual addon.
- Use `formatOptions` for localisation rather than manually adding currency symbols to the stored value.

Formatting follows the `BreezeProvider` locale and the surrounding direction. Right-to-left layout reverses visual ordering appropriately while the number remains locale formatted.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not store the formatted text, use a number field for an identifier, pass numeric state to `Input`, or omit step-button names. Be careful with binary floating-point for money; convert to an appropriate minor-unit or decimal representation at the domain boundary. Use `Slider` for bounded direct manipulation, `TextField` for numeric-looking strings, and `InputGroup` for a purely visual unit when locale formatting is not appropriate.
fields-numberfield--docs.summary: Locale-aware numeric entry with optional increment and decrement controls, explicit constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and validation states.

primitives-overlays-popover--docs.name: Docs
primitives-overlays-popover--docs.title: Primitives/Overlays/Popover
primitives-overlays-popover--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Popover.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A trigger-anchored contextual dialog with required title and description, modal or nonmodal interaction, collision-aware placement, and controlled or uncontrolled open state."
/>

# Popover

`Popover` places lightweight interactive content next to the control that requested it.

## Import

```tsx
import { Popover } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for contextual filters, settings, or explanations containing interactive controls. Use `Tooltip` for concise non-interactive help, `Menu` for a list of actions, `Dialog` for a focused modal task, and inline content when it should remain continuously visible.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Popover } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Popover.Root>
  <Popover.Trigger>Show details</Popover.Trigger>
  <Popover.Content>
    <Popover.Title>Details</Popover.Title>
    <Popover.Description>
      Supporting information about this item.
    </Popover.Description>
    <Popover.Close>Done</Popover.Close>
  </Popover.Content>
</Popover.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.NonmodalOutsideDismissal} />

## Anatomy and interaction modes

| Part                  | Purpose                                            |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `Popover.Root`        | Coordinates open state and trigger focus.          |
| `Popover.Trigger`     | Opens the popover from a semantic button.          |
| `Popover.Content`     | Positioned dialog surface and dismissal boundary.  |
| `Popover.Title`       | Required accessible name.                          |
| `Popover.Description` | Required accessible description.                   |
| `Popover.Close`       | Explicit close action that restores trigger focus. |

Content is nonmodal by default, so users may interact outside and outside activation dismisses it. Set `modal` when interaction must remain inside. `dismissible` defaults to `true`; `keyboardDismissDisabled` defaults to `false`. Always retain a clear close path if either dismissal route is removed.

## State and placement

Use uncontrolled `defaultOpen`, controlled `open` with `onOpenChange`, or immutable `open` with `readOnly`. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean.

Placement defaults to `'bottom start'` and supports `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`, and top/bottom start/end combinations. React Aria flips or shifts the surface when space is constrained. `offset` is the trigger gap in pixels and defaults to `8`. Start/end and collision behaviour follow provider direction.

Loading, disabled, invalid, empty, and error states belong to controls or feedback within Content. A disabled Trigger cannot open the popover. Do not show an empty surface.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and callback semantics

Content accepts relevant dialog HTML and ARIA attributes. Use `onAction` on Trigger and Close rather than native click handlers. Breeze owns portal, positioning, role, and inline style behaviour.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and mistakes

Trigger activation opens Content and moves focus appropriately. Tab follows modal or nonmodal focus rules, Escape closes unless disabled, and Close restores focus. Title and Description are required even when the trigger appears descriptive. Ensure the surface remains understandable when translated text wraps and test preferred placement in RTL.

Do not use a Popover as a tooltip, omit its accessible anatomy, place a long workflow inside it, or assume preferred placement is guaranteed. Related components: `Tooltip`, `Menu`, `Dialog`, `Select`, and `ComboBox`.
primitives-overlays-popover--docs.summary: A trigger-anchored contextual dialog with required title and description, modal or nonmodal interaction, collision-aware placement, and controlled or uncontrolled open state.

feedback-progressbar--docs.name: Docs
feedback-progressbar--docs.title: Feedback/ProgressBar
feedback-progressbar--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './ProgressBar.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled determinate or indeterminate indicator for the progress of an ongoing operation."
/>

# ProgressBar

`ProgressBar` communicates how far an operation has progressed, or that its progress cannot yet be measured.

```tsx
import { ProgressBar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for time-based operation progress such as uploading or processing. Use [Meter](?path=/docs/feedback-meter--docs) for a stable measurement such as storage used, [Spinner](?path=/docs/feedback-spinner--docs) for a compact indeterminate activity mark, and [Stepper](?path=/docs/progression-stepper--docs) for named stages.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { ProgressBar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function UploadProgress({ percentage }: { percentage: number }) {
  return (
    <ProgressBar
      label="Uploading files"
      value={percentage}
      valueText={`${percentage} percent complete`}
    />
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Determinate} />

## Determinate and indeterminate state

Determinate progress requires `value`; `minimum` defaults to `0`, `maximum` to `100`, and `indeterminate` is `false`. Set `indeterminate` to `true` and omit both `value` and `valueText` when no defensible measurement exists. Switch to determinate only when a meaningful value is available.

`valueText` replaces the numeric value announced by assistive technology. Author and translate it when units or range meaning are not obvious. Values are interpreted within the supplied numeric range.

<Canvas of={Stories.Indeterminate} />

## Variants and accessibility

`variant` is `primary` by default and accepts `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, or `dark`. Use semantic colour only as supporting information. The visible translated `label` is required and labels the progressbar. The component has no keyboard interaction, callback, disabled, invalid, empty, or error state; keep operation cancellation in a separate `Button`. Direction is inherited, and formatting application-specific values remains the consumer's responsibility.

`ProgressBar` supports relevant native `div` attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; `children`, `role`, and inline `style` are intentionally owned or excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not use a progress bar for a static scalar measurement.
- Do not invent percentages for an indeterminate operation.
- Do not omit or visually hide the required operation label.
- Do not announce frequent progress changes elsewhere as well.

## Related components

[Meter](?path=/docs/feedback-meter--docs), [Spinner](?path=/docs/feedback-spinner--docs), [Stepper](?path=/docs/progression-stepper--docs), and [Skeleton](?path=/docs/feedback-skeleton--docs).

selection-radiogroup--docs.name: Docs
selection-radiogroup--docs.title: Selection/RadioGroup
selection-radiogroup--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './RadioGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled exclusive selection with native radio form semantics, per-option guidance, orientation-aware keyboard navigation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states."
/>

# RadioGroup

`RadioGroup` presents a visible set of options from which one string value may be selected.

## Import

```tsx
import { RadioGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when choices are mutually exclusive, the set is short, and comparing all options helps the decision. Prefer `Select` when space is constrained or there are many options, `CheckboxGroup` when several values may be selected, and `SegmentedControl` for a small view or mode switch. Do not use radio buttons for an action that takes effect without representing a persisted choice.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { RadioGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<RadioGroup.Root name="frequency" defaultSelection="weekly">
  <RadioGroup.Label>Report frequency</RadioGroup.Label>
  <RadioGroup.Item value="daily">
    <RadioGroup.Control>
      <RadioGroup.Indicator />
      <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Daily</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
    </RadioGroup.Control>
  </RadioGroup.Item>
  <RadioGroup.Item value="weekly">
    <RadioGroup.Control>
      <RadioGroup.Indicator />
      <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Weekly</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
    </RadioGroup.Control>
  </RadioGroup.Item>
</RadioGroup.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndHorizontalKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                         | Purpose                                                                   |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `RadioGroup.Root`            | Owns exclusive selection, shared form name, orientation, and group state. |
| `RadioGroup.Label`           | Persistent accessible name for the set.                                   |
| `RadioGroup.Item`            | One keyed option with its stable string `value`.                          |
| `RadioGroup.Control`         | Native label and interaction target for one option.                       |
| `RadioGroup.Indicator`       | Decorative selected-state mark.                                           |
| `RadioGroup.ItemLabel`       | Persistent visible name inside the control.                               |
| `RadioGroup.ItemDescription` | Guidance associated with one option.                                      |
| `RadioGroup.Description`     | Guidance associated with the whole set.                                   |
| `RadioGroup.Error`           | Group validation feedback while invalid.                                  |

Put `Control` and optional `ItemDescription` inside `Item`; put `Indicator` and `ItemLabel` inside `Control`. Keep group-level guidance outside items.

## Selection, callbacks, and states

Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for an immutable choice. `selection` is `string | null`; `null` means no selection. `onSelectionChange` receives the newly selected string.

`disabled` on `Root` disables every option; `disabled` on `Item` disables one. `readOnly` prevents changes to a controlled selection. `required` means an option must be selected. Set `invalid` and render `Error` for a group validation failure. Loading and an empty option set are application-owned; avoid rendering an empty radio group.

`orientation` is `vertical` by default. Use `horizontal` only for short labels and a small set; it also sets the expected arrow-key axis.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab enters the group at the selected option, or the first enabled option when none is selected.
- Arrow keys move focus and selection through enabled options according to orientation; disabled items are skipped.
- Space selects the focused option where platform behaviour requires it.
- Provide both a group `Label` and an `ItemLabel` for every option. Do not rely on colour or the indicator alone.
- Stable `value` strings should be domain identifiers, not translated visible labels.

The component follows surrounding `dir`; horizontal arrow behaviour and layout adapt for right-to-left content.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not give options duplicate values, put `value` on `Control`, omit the group label, or use horizontal layout for content that wraps ambiguously. Use `Select` for a compact list, `CheckboxGroup` for multiple selection, and `SegmentedControl` for a compact mode choice.
selection-radiogroup--docs.summary: Labelled exclusive selection with native radio form semantics, per-option guidance, orientation-aware keyboard navigation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states.

date-and-time-rangecalendar--docs.name: Docs
date-and-time-rangecalendar--docs.title: Date and Time/RangeCalendar
date-and-time-rangecalendar--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './RangeCalendar.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Always-visible locale-aware calendar for selecting an inclusive start and end date as stable YYYY-MM-DD strings, with compound navigation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# RangeCalendar

`RangeCalendar` presents one or more month grids for selecting an inclusive start and end date.

## Import

```tsx
import { RangeCalendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `RangeCalendar` when comparing dates visually is central and the calendar should remain visible. Use `DateRangePicker` for a compact labelled form control, `Calendar` for one date, and two independent `DateField` instances only when the dates are not one coherent range.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { RangeCalendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<RangeCalendar.Root defaultValue={{ start: '2026-07-20', end: '2026-07-24' }}>
  <RangeCalendar.Header>
    <RangeCalendar.PreviousButton />
    <RangeCalendar.Heading />
    <RangeCalendar.NextButton />
  </RangeCalendar.Header>
  <RangeCalendar.Grid />
</RangeCalendar.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.UncontrolledRange} />

## Anatomy

| Part                           | Purpose                                                    |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `RangeCalendar.Root`           | Owns range selection, visible months and date constraints. |
| `RangeCalendar.Header`         | Lays out navigation and the visible-period heading.        |
| `RangeCalendar.PreviousButton` | Moves to the previous calendar page.                       |
| `RangeCalendar.Heading`        | Labels the current visible period.                         |
| `RangeCalendar.NextButton`     | Moves to the next calendar page.                           |
| `RangeCalendar.Grid`           | Renders weekdays, days and range-selection semantics.      |

## Range behaviour and state

A complete value is `{ start: 'YYYY-MM-DD', end: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }`; both endpoints are inclusive and `start` must not follow `end`. Empty state is `null`. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives a complete stable range after selection.

By default, an unavailable date blocks a range that crosses it. Set `allowsNonContiguousRanges` only when the domain explicitly permits a range containing unavailable days. `isDateUnavailable(date, anchorDate)` receives the candidate ISO date and the current range-selection anchor, or `null` before selection begins. `minValue` and `maxValue` are inclusive boundaries.

`disabled` prevents focus and selection. `invalid` exposes an unacceptable selection but does not supply prose; render application-owned error text nearby. Read-only preserves navigation and inspection while preventing changes. Loading and persistence are application-owned.

## Keyboard, accessibility and internationalisation

- Arrow keys move focus by day or week; Home and End move within a week; Page Up and Page Down change the visible month. Enter or Space sets the range start and then its inclusive end.
- Keep `Heading` and navigation controls in the root so the grid has a clear visible period and operable paging.
- Week layout, weekday names, announcements and date formatting follow the provider locale. Directional navigation and icons follow provider direction.
- Range meaning must not rely only on background colour. Breeze exposes selected range states to assistive technology.
- Use `weekdayStyle="short"` by default; `long` needs more width and `narrow` can be ambiguous.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use two unrelated scalar strings, exclusive-end semantics, date objects or locale-formatted values. Do not enable non-contiguous ranges merely to bypass an unavailable-date rule. Use `DateRangePicker` in forms, `Calendar` for one date, and `DateTimePicker` when each selection represents an instant rather than a calendar day.

fields-searchfield--docs.name: Docs
fields-searchfield--docs.title: Fields/SearchField
fields-searchfield--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './SearchField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled search query entry with explicit submission, conditional clearing, controlled and uncontrolled values, and accessible field states."
/>

# SearchField

`SearchField` is a compound query input with search semantics, submission, and an optional clear action.

## Import

```tsx
import { SearchField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when entered text filters or searches content and users benefit from clearing the whole query. Prefer `TextField` for ordinary data entry and `ComboBox` when the input suggests or selects options from a collection. Do not use `SearchField` as a generic input merely to obtain its clear button.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { SearchField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<SearchField.Root onSubmit={(query) => runSearch(query)}>
  <SearchField.Label>Search records</SearchField.Label>
  <SearchField.Group>
    <SearchField.Input name="query" placeholder="Name or reference" />
    <SearchField.ClearButton />
  </SearchField.Group>
  <SearchField.Description>Enter one or more keywords.</SearchField.Description>
</SearchField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                      | Purpose                                                                             |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SearchField.Root`        | Owns query value, search submission, clearing, and field state.                     |
| `SearchField.Label`       | Persistently names the query input.                                                 |
| `SearchField.Group`       | Presents the input and clear action as one control and synchronises their size.     |
| `SearchField.Input`       | Renders a native `type="search"` input.                                             |
| `SearchField.ClearButton` | Clears a non-empty mutable query; it is omitted when empty, disabled, or read-only. |
| `SearchField.Description` | Associates query guidance.                                                          |
| `SearchField.Error`       | Associates validation feedback while invalid.                                       |

`Input` can be used without `Group`, but the canonical composition places `Input` and `ClearButton` inside `Group`.

## Value, callbacks, and states

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next query string. `onSubmit` receives the current query when the user submits. `onClear` runs after the value is cleared by the clear button or Escape.

`disabled` removes interaction. `readOnly` keeps the query immutable and hides the clear action. `invalid`, `required`, `Description`, and `Error` follow the `TextField` contract. An empty query is `''`; `ClearButton` does not render for it. The application owns loading, results, empty-result messages, errors, and request cancellation.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses the search input and then any rendered clear button.
- Enter invokes `onSubmit` with the current query.
- Escape clears a non-empty mutable query and invokes `onClear`; native browser behaviour may require a second Escape to move focus or close an ancestor overlay.
- The clear button uses the provider’s localised `clear` message by default. Supply `aria-label` when product wording needs to be more specific.
- Provide a persistent `Label`. Announce result counts and asynchronous states in the result region, not by repeatedly changing the field label.
- Search results should have a clear heading or live-region strategy and sensible focus behaviour; `SearchField` does not render results.

`Group` supports `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`, applying the size to its input and clear action. It follows surrounding writing direction and uses logical layout. Search strings are ordinary Unicode strings.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not manage clearing with a second button outside `Root`, assume `onSubmit` starts a request automatically, or display a clear button for read-only content. Use `TextField` for ordinary text, `ComboBox` for suggestions and option selection, and `ListBox` for a persistent result collection.
fields-searchfield--docs.summary: Labelled search query entry with explicit submission, conditional clearing, controlled and uncontrolled values, and accessible field states.

collections-select--docs.name: Docs
collections-select--docs.title: Collections/Select
collections-select--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Select.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Compact single selection from static or typed keyed options with a trigger, popover listbox, controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, empty, and native form states."
/>

# Select

`Select` provides compact single selection from a known collection of keyed options.

## Import

```tsx
import { Select } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when users must choose one known value and keeping every option visible would consume too much space. Prefer `RadioGroup` for a short comparison-sensitive set, `ComboBox` when users need filtering or free-form entry, and `ListBox` for persistent or multiple selection. Do not use Select for navigation; use links, menus, or a navigation component.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Select } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Select.Root name="region" placeholder="Choose a region">
  <Select.Label>Region</Select.Label>
  <Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Value />
  </Select.Trigger>
  <Select.Popover>
    <Select.ListBox>
      <Select.Item id="north" textValue="North">
        North
      </Select.Item>
      <Select.Item id="south" textValue="South">
        South
      </Select.Item>
    </Select.ListBox>
  </Select.Popover>
</Select.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Select.Root`        | Owns selected key, validation, placeholder, and native form value. |
| `Select.Label`       | Persistently names the select.                                     |
| `Select.Trigger`     | Opens and closes the popup and contains the displayed value.       |
| `Select.Value`       | Displays the selected option text or root placeholder.             |
| `Select.Popover`     | Positions option content relative to the trigger.                  |
| `Select.ListBox`     | Renders static or typed generic options and the empty state.       |
| `Select.Item`        | One option with a stable string or number key and `textValue`.     |
| `Select.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the select.                    |
| `Select.Error`       | Validation feedback while invalid.                                 |

Keep `Value` inside `Trigger`, and `ListBox` inside `Popover`. A `ListBox` item’s `id` is its public value; visible content can be localised independently.

## Collections and selection

Static collections contain authored `Select.Item` children. Dynamic collections pass `items`, where each item has a stable `id: string | number`, and a child renderer.

```tsx
<Select.ListBox items={regions}>
  {(region) => (
    <Select.Item id={region.id} textValue={region.name}>
      {region.name}
    </Select.Item>
  )}
</Select.ListBox>
```

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable selection. The value is `string | number | null`; `null` means no option. `onChange` receives the next key or `null`.

`disabled` prevents focus and selection. `readOnly` is implemented as non-interactive while retaining the controlled value. `required` and `invalid` expose validation semantics. `emptyContent` defaults to `'No options'`. Loading options is application-owned; do not replace the collection without preserving stable keys and a clear status.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the trigger. Enter, Space, or Arrow Down opens the listbox; Escape closes it.
- Arrow keys move the active option, typeahead matches each item’s `textValue`, and Enter or Space selects according to platform behaviour.
- The popup returns focus to the trigger when dismissed.
- Always provide `Label`; provide accurate `textValue` when option content is not plain text.
- `Trigger` supports `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`.
- Layout follows surrounding `dir`; keys remain stable while labels and empty content may be translated.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use array indexes as keys, omit `textValue` for visual option content, place links or buttons inside options, or use a Select to navigate. Use `ComboBox` for filtering, `RadioGroup` for visible comparison, and `ListBox` for persistent or multiple selection.
collections-select--docs.summary: Compact single selection from static or typed keyed options with a trigger, popover listbox, controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, empty, and native form states.

layout-separator--docs.name: Docs
layout-separator--docs.title: Layout/Separator
layout-separator--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Separator.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A semantic or decorative divider with horizontal and vertical orientations."
/>

# Separator

`Separator` draws a horizontal or vertical division and exposes separator semantics unless explicitly decorative.

## Import

```tsx
import { Separator } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a semantic separator when the division helps users understand neighbouring regions. Use `decorative` when the same relationship is already clear from structure and the line is visual only. Prefer `gap` on `Stack` or `Inline` when you only need spacing, and use headings or separate landmarks for stronger structural boundaries.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Separator, Stack, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function Sections() {
  return (
    <Stack>
      <Typography>Current details</Typography>
      <Separator />
      <Typography>Previous details</Typography>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Horizontal} />

## Orientation and semantics

Horizontal separators render an `hr`; vertical separators render a `div` with `role="separator"` and `aria-orientation="vertical"`. A vertical separator must be placed in a parent with a meaningful height. `decorative` changes the role to `presentation` and adds `aria-hidden="true"`.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility and keyboard behaviour

Relevant native attributes such as `id`, `aria-*`, and `data-*` are accepted. `children`, `role`, `aria-orientation`, and inline `style` are intentionally component-owned or unavailable. There are no callback, controlled, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error states.

Meaningful separators are discoverable but not focusable and have no keyboard interaction. Do not add an accessible name unless it disambiguates a genuinely meaningful divider. Direction does not change the declared horizontal or vertical axis.

## Common mistakes

- Do not expose repetitive visual rules as semantic separators.
- Do not mark a meaningful structural division `decorative`.
- Do not use a vertical separator without height-bearing surrounding layout.

## Related components

Use `Stack` and `Inline` to place separators, `Surface` for a bounded region, and `Accordion` or `Disclosure` when the boundary also controls visibility.
layout-separator--docs.summary: A semantic or decorative divider with horizontal and vertical orientations.

feedback-skeleton--docs.name: Docs
feedback-skeleton--docs.title: Feedback/Skeleton
feedback-skeleton--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Skeleton.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Non-announcing visual placeholder that reserves loading layout with text, circle, or rectangle geometry."
/>

# Skeleton

`Skeleton` reserves the shape of content while that content is loading without adding placeholder noise to the accessibility tree.

```tsx
import { Skeleton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use skeletons when the eventual layout is predictable and preventing layout shift helps orientation. Use [Spinner](?path=/docs/feedback-spinner--docs) for a compact activity mark, [ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs) for measurable work, or [StatePanel](?path=/docs/patterns-feedback-statepanel--docs) when the whole region has a meaningful loading or empty message.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Skeleton, Stack } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function LoadingSummary() {
  return (
    <section aria-busy="true" aria-label="Loading summary">
      <Stack gap="sm">
        <Skeleton className="w-2/3" />
        <Skeleton />
        <Skeleton className="min-h-32" shape="rectangle" />
      </Stack>
    </section>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Shapes} />

## Shapes, element, and tone

`shape` is `text` by default; `circle` provides a canonical avatar-sized placeholder and `rectangle` provides a minimum block height. Adjust width or height with `className` to mirror the real content. `as` defaults to `div`; use `as="span"` only when inline placement requires valid markup.

`tone` accepts `default`, `inverse`, or `danger` and defaults to `default`. Match tone to the host surface rather than using it as a data status. Motion is suppressed for reduced-motion preferences.

## Accessibility and state

Every skeleton has `aria-hidden="true"`, has no role, and accepts no children. Put `aria-busy="true"` and a translated accessible label or status on the owning content region where users need loading context. Skeleton has no callback, keyboard interaction, controlled value, disabled, invalid, empty, or error behaviour. Remove placeholders when real content or an error appears. Geometry and logical placement work in both directions.

`Skeleton` supports relevant attributes for its selected `div` or `span`, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; `children`, `role`, and inline `style` are intentionally owned or excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not expose fake placeholder text to assistive technology.
- Do not use arbitrary shapes unrelated to the eventual layout.
- Do not leave a loading region permanently busy after an error.
- Do not use a skeleton as a disabled control or content-redaction treatment.

## Related components

[Spinner](?path=/docs/feedback-spinner--docs), [ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs), and [StatePanel](?path=/docs/patterns-feedback-statepanel--docs).
feedback-skeleton--docs.summary: Non-announcing visual placeholder that reserves loading layout with text, circle, or rectangle geometry.

primitives-navigation-skiplink--docs.name: Docs
primitives-navigation-skiplink--docs.title: Primitives/Navigation/SkipLink
primitives-navigation-skiplink--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './SkipLink.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A focus-revealed native fragment link that lets keyboard users bypass repeated page chrome."
/>

# SkipLink

`SkipLink` is the first keyboard destination on a page and moves users directly to its main content target.

## Import

```tsx
import { SkipLink } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use one near the beginning of every application shell with repeated navigation. It complements landmarks and headings; it is not a replacement for them. Use ordinary `Link` for visible navigation.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { SkipLink } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<>
  <SkipLink targetId="main-content">Skip to main content</SkipLink>
  <header>{/* Repeated navigation */}</header>
  <main id="main-content" tabIndex={-1}>
    Page content
  </main>
</>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.KeyboardFocus} />

## Behaviour

`targetId` is written without `#`; Breeze creates a native `#fragment` link. The matching target must exist and should be a meaningful landmark. Add `tabIndex={-1}` where the target is not naturally focusable so browsers can move focus consistently. The link is visually off-screen until focused and respects reduced-motion preferences.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and states

Relevant native anchor attributes such as `aria-*`, `data-*`, and `hreflang` are supported. `href`, inline style, and click handlers are owned by Breeze. SkipLink has no disabled, loading, controlled, read-only, invalid, empty, or error state.

## Accessibility, internationalisation, and mistakes

Place it before all repeated focusable chrome. Tab reveals and focuses it; Enter follows the fragment. Use a concise translated action such as “Skip to main content”, keep the target id stable across locales, and test that focus lands visibly at the intended heading or region in both directions.

Do not use a missing or duplicated target id, put the link after navigation, suppress its focus styling, or route fragment activation through application navigation. Related components: `ApplicationShell`, `NavigationList`, `Link`, and `VisuallyHidden`.
primitives-navigation-skiplink--docs.summary: A focus-revealed native fragment link that lets keyboard users bypass repeated page chrome.

selection-slider--docs.name: Docs
selection-slider--docs.title: Selection/Slider
selection-slider--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Slider.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible single-value range input with explicit label, formatted output, track, fill, thumb, guidance, validation, horizontal or vertical orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, and read-only state."
/>

# Slider

`Slider` selects one numeric value in a bounded range through pointer, touch, or keyboard interaction.

## Import

```tsx
import { Slider } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when relative position and quick adjustment matter more than typing an exact value, such as volume or intensity. Prefer `NumberField` for exact numeric entry, unbounded values, or values where comparing digits is essential. Do not use a slider when the range or current value cannot be explained clearly.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Slider } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Slider.Root defaultValue={40} min={0} max={100} step={5}>
  <Slider.Label>Volume</Slider.Label>
  <Slider.Output />
  <Slider.Track>
    <Slider.Fill />
    <Slider.Thumb name="volume" />
  </Slider.Track>
  <Slider.Description>Adjust from 0 to 100.</Slider.Description>
</Slider.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Horizontal} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Slider.Root`        | Owns value, range, step, orientation, formatting, and field state. |
| `Slider.Label`       | Persistently names the slider.                                     |
| `Slider.Output`      | Displays the locale-formatted current value or custom content.     |
| `Slider.Track`       | Pointer and touch interaction surface.                             |
| `Slider.Fill`        | Decorative selected portion of the track.                          |
| `Slider.Thumb`       | Draggable focus target and hidden native range input.              |
| `Slider.Description` | Guidance associated with the thumb.                                |
| `Slider.Error`       | Validation feedback associated with the thumb while invalid.       |

Keep `Fill` and `Thumb` inside `Track`; keep all parts inside `Root`. This API is single-value only: render another component if the domain needs a range with two thumbs.

## Value and callbacks

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives each next number during interaction. `onChangeEnd` receives the final number after a drag or keyboard sequence, which is useful for an expensive commit while `onChange` keeps the interface responsive.

`min` defaults to `0`, `max` to `100`, and `step` to `1`. `formatOptions` accepts `Intl.NumberFormatOptions`; `Output` uses it and may be replaced with custom children when the same value meaning remains accessible. `disabled` prevents focus and changes. `readOnly` disables the thumb while preserving the controlled value. `invalid` and `required` are applied to the hidden range input; render `Error` for recovery guidance. Loading is application-owned.

## Orientation, size, and formatting

`orientation` defaults to `horizontal`; vertical layout provides a 256-pixel track. `Thumb` sizes are `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`. Choose a step that divides the meaningful domain and avoid excessive precision. Use locale formatting for percentages, units, or currency rather than manually concatenating symbols.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses the thumb. Arrow keys change by `step`; Page Up and Page Down make larger changes; Home and End move to minimum and maximum.
- Pointer or touch on the track moves the value; dragging the thumb updates continuously.
- Provide `Label`. `aria-label` on `Thumb` is available when the root label cannot sufficiently name the thumb, but is usually unnecessary for a single-value slider.
- Communicate the range and unit in `Description` when they are not obvious. Do not rely only on position or colour.
- Layout, track direction, keyboard interpretation, and number formatting follow locale and surrounding `dir`.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use Slider for exact entry, omit a visible or accessible value, choose meaningless fractional steps, or perform expensive persistence on every `onChange` when `onChangeEnd` suffices. Use `NumberField` for exact numbers, `ProgressBar` for read-only progress, and `Meter` for a read-only measurement.
selection-slider--docs.summary: Accessible single-value range input with explicit label, formatted output, track, fill, thumb, guidance, validation, horizontal or vertical orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, and read-only state.

feedback-spinner--docs.name: Docs
feedback-spinner--docs.title: Feedback/Spinner
feedback-spinner--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Spinner.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Compact indeterminate activity indicator that is either explicitly labelled or deliberately decorative."
/>

# Spinner

`Spinner` indicates ongoing activity when the duration cannot be measured.

```tsx
import { Spinner } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a spinner for compact indeterminate activity, especially inside an existing status layout. Use [ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs) when progress is measurable, [Skeleton](?path=/docs/feedback-skeleton--docs) to reserve a loading layout, and a disabled/loading-capable control pattern when activity belongs to one action.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Inline, Spinner, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function SavingStatus() {
  return (
    <Inline gap="sm" wrap={false}>
      <Spinner />
      <Typography as="span">Saving changes</Typography>
    </Inline>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Decorative} />

## Labelling

Omit `label` when adjacent visible text already communicates the activity. The spinner then has `aria-hidden="true"` and is purely decorative. Supply an application-owned translated `label` when it is the only status; Breeze renders `role="status"` and a visually hidden accessible name.

Do not supply both a spinner label and duplicate live status text. The animation stops under reduced-motion preferences while the status semantics remain.

## Sizes and variants

`size` accepts `sm`, `md`, or `lg` and defaults to `md`. `variant` accepts `primary`, `secondary`, `success`, `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `light`, or `dark` and defaults to `primary`. Select `light` only on a sufficiently dark surface and `dark` only on a light surface.

<Canvas of={Stories.SizesAndVariants} />

## State, keyboard, and direction

Spinner is indeterminate and read-only. It has no callback, keyboard interaction, disabled, invalid, empty, or error state. Render or remove it as application state changes. Its circular motion is direction-independent. Translate `label`; do not add directional copy to the decorative mark.

`Spinner` supports relevant native `span` attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; `children`, `role`, and inline `style` are intentionally owned or excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not show an unlabelled standalone spinner with no visible status.
- Do not announce the same status twice.
- Do not use a spinner for measurable progress or as a permanent decoration.
- Do not rely on animation alone to communicate loading.

## Related components

[ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs), [Skeleton](?path=/docs/feedback-skeleton--docs), and [Alert](?path=/docs/feedback-alert--docs).
feedback-spinner--docs.summary: Compact indeterminate activity indicator that is either explicitly labelled or deliberately decorative.

layout-stack--docs.name: Docs
layout-stack--docs.title: Layout/Stack
layout-stack--docs.content:
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Stack.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A vertical layout with responsive, token-based spacing and bounded alignment."
/>

# Stack

`Stack` arranges related children vertically while preserving their authored reading order.

## Import

```tsx
import { Stack } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `Stack` for forms, page sections, cards, and other vertical flows whose spacing should use Breeze tokens. Use `Inline` for a horizontal flow, `Grid` for columns, or ordinary semantic HTML when no layout abstraction is needed.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Button, Stack, Typography } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function EmptyMessage() {
  return (
    <Stack align="start" gap="md">
      <Typography as="h2" level="heading-md">
        No saved views
      </Typography>
      <Typography>Create a view to reuse these filters.</Typography>
      <Button>Create view</Button>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Default} />

## Layout and responsive spacing

`align` controls the horizontal cross-axis; `justify` controls distribution on the vertical main axis. `gap` accepts `none`, `xs`, `sm`, `control`, `compact`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`, `xxl`, or `page`, either directly or as `{ base, sm?, md?, lg? }`. Breakpoints start at 681 px, 901 px, and 1181 px.

```tsx
<Stack gap={{ base: 'sm', md: 'xl' }}>
  <section>First</section>
  <section>Second</section>
</Stack>
```

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Accessibility, direction, and content

Relevant native `div` attributes such as `id`, `aria-*`, and `data-*` are supported; inline `style` is not. `Stack` has no selection, callback, loading, invalid, disabled, controlled, or read-only state.

`Stack` is a neutral `div` and adds no role, label, focus, or keyboard interaction. Use semantic children and preserve a logical DOM order. It remains vertical in both left-to-right and right-to-left layouts. Empty stacks render but usually indicate that conditional rendering belongs to the parent.

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `justify="between"` unless the stack has a meaningful available height.
- Do not use `align="center"` for long-form content that should remain easy to scan.
- Do not add margins to every child; set `gap` on the stack.

## Related components

Choose `Inline` for rows, `Grid` for two-dimensional layouts, `Center` for one centred composition, and `Separator` when a visible or semantic division is required.
layout-stack--docs.summary: A vertical layout with responsive, token-based spacing and bounded alignment.
